rules.title: Potential Azure Browser SSO Abuse
rules.description:
Detects abusing Azure Browser SSO by requesting OAuth 2.0 refresh tokens for an Azure-AD-authenticated Windows user (i.e. the machine is joined to Azure AD and a user logs in with their Azure AD account) wanting to perform SSO authentication in the browser.
An attacker can use this to authenticate to Azure AD in a browser as that user.

rules.title: CredUI.DLL Loaded By Uncommon Process

rules.title: Suspicious Unsigned Dbghelp/Dbgcore DLL Loaded
rules.description:
Detects the load of dbghelp/dbgcore DLL (used to make memory dumps) by suspicious processes.
Tools like ProcessHacker and some attacker tradecract use MiniDumpWriteDump API found in dbghelp.dll or dbgcore.dll.
As an example, SilentTrynity C2 Framework has a module that leverages this API to dump the contents of Lsass.exe and transfer it over the network back to the attacker's machine.

rules.title: PCRE.NET Package Image Load

rules.title: Load Of RstrtMgr.DLL By A Suspicious Process
rules.description:
Detects the load of RstrtMgr DLL (Restart Manager) by a suspicious process.
This library has been used during ransomware campaigns to kill processes that would prevent file encryption by locking them (e.g. Conti ransomware, Cactus ransomware). It has also recently been seen used by the BiBi wiper for Windows.
It could also be used for anti-analysis purposes by shut downing specific processes.

rules.title: Load Of RstrtMgr.DLL By An Uncommon Process
rules.description:
Detects the load of RstrtMgr DLL (Restart Manager) by an uncommon process.
This library has been used during ransomware campaigns to kill processes that would prevent file encryption by locking them (e.g. Conti ransomware, Cactus ransomware). It has also recently been seen used by the BiBi wiper for Windows.
It could also be used for anti-analysis purposes by shut downing specific processes.

rules.title: PowerShell Core DLL Loaded By Non PowerShell Process
rules.description:

rules.title: Potential DLL Sideloading Of Non-Existent DLLs From System Folders
rules.description:
Detects loading of specific system DLL files that are usually not present on the system (or at least not in system directories) but may be loaded by legitimate processes, potentially indicating phantom DLL hijacking attempts.
Phantom DLL hijacking involves placing malicious DLLs with names of non-existent system binaries in locations where legitimate applications may search for them, leading to execution of the malicious DLLs.

rules.title: Python Image Load By Non-Python Process
rules.description:
Detects the image load of "Python Core" by a non-Python process. This might be indicative of a execution of executable that has been bundled from Python code.
Various tools like Py2Exe, PyInstaller, and cx_Freeze are used to bundle Python code into standalone executables.
Threat actors often use these tools to bundle malicious Python scripts into executables, sometimes to obfuscate the code or to bypass security measures.

rules.title: DotNet CLR DLL Loaded By Scripting Applications
rules.description: Detects .NET CLR DLLs being loaded by scripting applications such as wscript or cscript. This could be an indication of potential suspicious execution.

rules.title: Unsigned DLL Loaded by Windows Utility
rules.description:
Detects windows utilities loading an unsigned or untrusted DLL.
Adversaries often abuse those programs to proxy execution of malicious code.

rules.title: Suspicious Unsigned Thor Scanner Execution
rules.description: Detects loading and execution of an unsigned thor scanner binary.

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using Iscsicpl - ImageLoad
rules.description: Detects the "iscsicpl.exe" UAC bypass technique that leverages a DLL Search Order hijacking technique to load a custom DLL's from temp or a any user controlled location in the users %PATH%

rules.title: UAC Bypass With Fake DLL
rules.description: Attempts to load dismcore.dll after dropping it

rules.title: WMI Persistence - Command Line Event Consumer
rules.description: Detects WMI command line event consumers

rules.title: WMIC Loading Scripting Libraries
rules.description:
Detects threat actors proxy executing code and bypassing application controls by leveraging wmic and the `/FORMAT` argument switch to download and execute an XSL file (i.e js, vbs, etc).
It could be an indicator of SquiblyTwo technique, which uses Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to execute malicious code.

rules.title: Wmiprvse Wbemcomn DLL Hijack
rules.description: Detects a threat actor creating a file named `wbemcomn.dll` in the `C:\Windows\System32\wbem\` directory over the network and loading it for a WMI DLL Hijack scenario.

rules.title: Network Connection Initiated By AddinUtil.EXE
rules.description:
Detects a network connection initiated by the Add-In deployment cache updating utility "AddInutil.exe".
This could indicate a potential command and control communication as this tool doesn't usually initiate network activity.

rules.title: Uncommon Connection to Active Directory Web Services
rules.description: Detects uncommon network connections to the Active Directory Web Services (ADWS) from processes not typically associated with ADWS management.

rules.title: Uncommon Network Connection Initiated By Certutil.EXE
rules.description:
Detects a network connection initiated by the certutil.exe utility.
Attackers can abuse the utility in order to download malware or additional payloads.

rules.title: Outbound Network Connection Initiated By Cmstp.EXE
rules.description:
Detects a network connection initiated by Cmstp.EXE
Its uncommon for "cmstp.exe" to initiate an outbound network connection. Investigate the source of such requests to determine if they are malicious.

rules.title: Outbound Network Connection Initiated By Microsoft Dialer
rules.description:
Detects outbound network connection initiated by Microsoft Dialer.
The Microsoft Dialer, also known as Phone Dialer, is a built-in utility application included in various versions of the Microsoft Windows operating system. Its primary function is to provide users with a graphical interface for managing phone calls via a modem or a phone line connected to the computer.
This is an outdated process in the current conext of it's usage and is a common target for info stealers for process injection, and is used to make C2 connections, common example is "Rhadamanthys"

rules.title: Network Connection Initiated To AzureWebsites.NET By Non-Browser Process
rules.description: Detects an initiated network connection by a non browser process on the system to "azurewebsites.net". The latter was often used by threat actors as a malware hosting and exfiltration site.

rules.title: New Connection Initiated To Potential Dead Drop Resolver Domain
rules.description:
Detects an executable, which is not an internet browser or known application, initiating network connections to legit popular websites, which were seen to be used as dead drop resolvers in previous attacks.
In this context attackers leverage known websites such as "facebook", "youtube", etc. In order to pass through undetected.

values: C:\Program Files\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection\

rules.title: Network Connection Initiated To DevTunnels Domain
rules.description: Detects network connections to Devtunnels domains initiated by a process on a system. Attackers can abuse that feature to establish a reverse shell or persistence on a machine.

rules.title: Suspicious Dropbox API Usage

rules.title: Suspicious Network Connection to IP Lookup Service APIs
rules.description: Detects external IP address lookups by non-browser processes via services such as "api.ipify.org". This could be indicative of potential post compromise internet test activity.

rules.title: Communication To LocaltoNet Tunneling Service Initiated
rules.description:
Detects an executable initiating a network connection to "LocaltoNet" tunneling sub-domains.
LocaltoNet is a reverse proxy that enables localhost services to be exposed to the Internet.
Attackers have been seen to use this service for command-and-control activities to bypass MFA and perimeter controls.

rules.title: Network Connection Initiated To Mega.nz
rules.description:
Detects a network connection initiated by a binary to "api.mega.co.nz".
Attackers were seen abusing file sharing websites similar to "mega.nz" in order to upload/download additional payloads.

rules.title: Process Initiated Network Connection To Ngrok Domain
rules.description:
Detects an executable initiating a network connection to "ngrok" domains.
Attackers were seen using this "ngrok" in order to store their second stage payloads and malware.
While communication with such domains can be legitimate, often times is a sign of either data exfiltration by malicious actors or additional download.

rules.title: Communication To Ngrok Tunneling Service Initiated
rules.description:
Detects an executable initiating a network connection to "ngrok" tunneling domains.
Attackers were seen using this "ngrok" in order to store their second stage payloads and malware.
While communication with such domains can be legitimate, often times is a sign of either data exfiltration by malicious actors or additional download.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Network Connection To Notion API

rules.title: Network Communication Initiated To Portmap.IO Domain

rules.title: Network Connection Initiated To Visual Studio Code Tunnels Domain

rules.title: Network Connection Initiated By Eqnedt32.EXE

rules.title: Network Connection Initiated By IMEWDBLD.EXE

This rule aims to detect traffic similar to one seen exploited in CVE-2021-42292.
This rule will require an initial baseline and tuning that is specific to your organization.

rules.title: Suspicious Get Information for SMB Share
rules.description:
Adversaries may look for folders and drives shared on remote systems as a means of identifying sources of information to gather as
a precursor for Collection and to identify potential systems of interest for Lateral Movement.
Networks often contain shared network drives and folders that enable users to access file directories on various systems across a network.

rules.title: Suspicious SSL Connection
rules.description: Adversaries may employ a known encryption algorithm to conceal command and control traffic rather than relying on any inherent protections provided by a communication protocol.

rules.title: Suspicious Start-Process PassThru
rules.description: Powershell use PassThru option to start in background

rules.title: Suspicious Unblock-File
rules.description: Remove the Zone.Identifier alternate data stream which identifies the file as downloaded from the internet.

rules.title: Replace Desktop Wallpaper by Powershell
rules.description:
An adversary may deface systems internal to an organization in an attempt to intimidate or mislead users.
This may take the form of modifications to internal websites, or directly to user systems with the replacement of the desktop wallpaper

rules.title: Powershell Suspicious Win32_PnPEntity
rules.description: Adversaries may attempt to gather information about attached peripheral devices and components connected to a computer system.

rules.title: Deletion of Volume Shadow Copies via WMI with PowerShell - PS Script
rules.description: Detects deletion of Windows Volume Shadow Copies with PowerShell code and Get-WMIObject. This technique is used by numerous ransomware families such as Sodinokibi/REvil

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell WindowStyle Option
rules.description:
Adversaries may use hidden windows to conceal malicious activity from the plain sight of users.
In some cases, windows that would typically be displayed when an application carries out an operation can be hidden

rules.title: PowerShell Write-EventLog Usage
rules.description: Detects usage of the "Write-EventLog" cmdlet with 'RawData' flag. The cmdlet can be levreage to write malicious payloads to the EventLog and then retrieve them later for later use

rules.title: Zip A Folder With PowerShell For Staging In Temp - PowerShell Script
rules.description:
Detects PowerShell scripts that make use of the "Compress-Archive" Cmdlet in order to compress folders and files where the output is stored in a potentially suspicious location that is used often by malware for exfiltration.
An adversary might compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.

rules.title: SyncAppvPublishingServer Execution to Bypass Powershell Restriction
rules.description: Detects SyncAppvPublishingServer process execution which usually utilized by adversaries to bypass PowerShell execution restrictions.

rules.title: Tamper Windows Defender Remove-MpPreference - ScriptBlockLogging
rules.description: Detects attempts to remove Windows Defender configuration using the 'MpPreference' cmdlet

rules.title: Tamper Windows Defender - ScriptBlockLogging
rules.description: Detects PowerShell scripts attempting to disable scheduled scanning and other parts of Windows Defender ATP or set default actions to allow.

rules.title: Testing Usage of Uncommonly Used Port
rules.description:
Adversaries may communicate using a protocol and port paring that are typically not associated.
For example, HTTPS over port 8088(Citation: Symantec Elfin Mar 2019) or port 587(Citation: Fortinet Agent Tesla April 2018) as opposed to the traditional port 443.

rules.title: Powershell Timestomp
rules.description:
Adversaries may modify file time attributes to hide new or changes to existing files.
Timestomping is a technique that modifies the timestamps of a file (the modify, access, create, and change times), often to mimic files that are in the same folder.

rules.title: User Discovery And Export Via Get-ADUser Cmdlet - PowerShell
rules.description: Detects usage of the Get-ADUser cmdlet to collect user information and output it to a file

rules.title: Potential Persistence Via PowerShell User Profile Using Add-Content
rules.description: Detects calls to "Add-Content" cmdlet in order to modify the content of the user profile and potentially adding suspicious commands for persistence

rules.title: Abuse of Service Permissions to Hide Services Via Set-Service - PS
rules.description: Detects usage of the "Set-Service" powershell cmdlet to configure a new SecurityDescriptor that allows a service to be hidden from other utilities such as "sc.exe", "Get-Service"...etc. (Works only in powershell 7)

rules.title: Veeam Backup Servers Credential Dumping Script Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of a PowerShell script that contains calls to the "Veeam.Backup" class, in order to dump stored credentials.

rules.title: Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets - ScriptBlock
rules.description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via PowerShell scriptblock logs

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Call To Win32_NTEventlogFile Class - PSScript
rules.description: Detects usage of the WMI class "Win32_NTEventlogFile" in a potentially suspicious way (delete, backup, change permissions, etc.) from a PowerShell script

rules.title: PowerShell WMI Win32_Product Install MSI
rules.description: Detects the execution of an MSI file using PowerShell and the WMI Win32_Product class

rules.title: Potential WinAPI Calls Via PowerShell Scripts
rules.description: Detects use of WinAPI functions in PowerShell scripts

rules.title: Windows Defender Exclusions Added - PowerShell
rules.description: Detects modifications to the Windows Defender configuration settings using PowerShell to add exclusions

rules.title: Windows Firewall Profile Disabled
rules.description: Detects when a user disables the Windows Firewall via a Profile to help evade defense.

rules.title: Winlogon Helper DLL
rules.description:
Winlogon.exe is a Windows component responsible for actions at logon/logoff as well as the secure attention sequence (SAS) triggered by Ctrl-Alt-Delete.
Registry entries in HKLM\Software[Wow6432Node]Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\ and HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\ are
used to manage additional helper programs and functionalities that support Winlogon. Malicious modifications to these Registry keys may cause Winlogon to
load and execute malicious DLLs and/or executables.

rules.title: Powershell WMI Persistence
rules.description: Adversaries may establish persistence and elevate privileges by executing malicious content triggered by a Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) event subscription.

rules.title: WMIC Unquoted Services Path Lookup - PowerShell
rules.description: Detects known WMI recon method to look for unquoted service paths, often used by pentest inside of powershell scripts attackers enum scripts

rules.title: WMImplant Hack Tool
rules.description: Detects parameters used by WMImplant

rules.title: Suspicious X509Enrollment - Ps Script
rules.description: Detect use of X509Enrollment

rules.title: Powershell XML Execute Command
rules.description:
Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.
PowerShell is a powerful interactive command-line interface and scripting environment included in the Windows operating system. (Citation: TechNet PowerShell)
Adversaries can use PowerShell to perform a number of actions, including discovery of information and execution of code

rules.title: 7Zip Compressing Dump Files
rules.description: Detects execution of 7z in order to compress a file with a ".dmp"/".dump" extension, which could be a step in a process of dump file exfiltration.

rules.title: Compress Data and Lock With Password for Exfiltration With 7-ZIP
rules.description: An adversary may compress or encrypt data that is collected prior to exfiltration using 3rd party utilities

rules.title: Potential DLL Injection Via AccCheckConsole
rules.description:
Detects the execution "AccCheckConsole" a command-line tool for verifying the accessibility implementation of an application's UI.
One of the tests that this checker can run are called "verification routine", which tests for things like Consistency, Navigation, etc.
The tool allows a user to provide a DLL that can contain a custom "verification routine". An attacker can build such DLLs and pass it via the CLI, which would then be loaded in the context of the "AccCheckConsole" utility.

rules.title: Suspicious AddinUtil.EXE CommandLine Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) with suspicious Addinroot or Pipelineroot paths. An adversary may execute AddinUtil.exe with uncommon Addinroot/Pipelineroot paths that point to the adversaries Addins.Store payload.

rules.title: Uncommon Child Process Of AddinUtil.EXE
rules.description: Detects uncommon child processes of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) which could be a sign of potential abuse of the binary to proxy execution via a custom Addins.Store payload.

rules.title: Uncommon AddinUtil.EXE CommandLine Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) with uncommon Addinroot or Pipelineroot paths. An adversary may execute AddinUtil.exe with uncommon Addinroot/Pipelineroot paths that point to the adversaries Addins.Store payload.

rules.title: AddinUtil.EXE Execution From Uncommon Directory
rules.description: Detects execution of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) from a non-standard directory.

rules.title: Potential Adplus.EXE Abuse
rules.description: Detects execution of "AdPlus.exe", a binary that is part of the Windows SDK that can be used as a LOLBIN in order to dump process memory and execute arbitrary commands.

rules.title: AgentExecutor PowerShell Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of the AgentExecutor.exe binary. Which can be abused as a LOLBIN to execute powershell scripts with the ExecutionPolicy "Bypass" or any binary named "powershell.exe" located in the path provided by 6th positional argument

rules.title: Suspicious AgentExecutor PowerShell Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of the AgentExecutor.exe binary. Which can be abused as a LOLBIN to execute powershell scripts with the ExecutionPolicy "Bypass" or any binary named "powershell.exe" located in the path provided by 6th positional argument

rules.title: Uncommon Child Process Of Appvlp.EXE
rules.description:
Detects uncommon child processes of Appvlp.EXE
Appvlp or the Application Virtualization Utility is included with Microsoft Office. Attackers are able to abuse "AppVLP" to execute shell commands.
Normally, this binary is used for Application Virtualization, but it can also be abused to circumvent the ASR file path rule folder
or to mark a file as a system file.

rules.title: AspNetCompiler Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of "aspnet_compiler.exe" which can be abused to compile and execute C# code.

rules.title: Suspicious Child Process of AspNetCompiler
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "aspnet_compiler.exe".

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious ASP.NET Compilation Via AspNetCompiler
rules.description: Detects execution of "aspnet_compiler.exe" with potentially suspicious paths for compilation.

rules.title: Interactive AT Job
rules.description: Detects an interactive AT job, which may be used as a form of privilege escalation.

rules.title: Uncommon  Assistive Technology Applications Execution Via AtBroker.EXE
rules.description: Detects the start of a non built-in assistive technology applications via "Atbroker.EXE".

rules.title: Hiding Files with Attrib.exe
rules.description: Detects usage of attrib.exe to hide files from users.

rules.title: Set Suspicious Files as System Files Using Attrib.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of attrib with the "+s" option to set scripts or executables located in suspicious locations as system files to hide them from users and make them unable to be deleted with simple rights. The rule limits the search to specific extensions and directories to avoid FPs

rules.title: Audit Policy Tampering Via NT Resource Kit Auditpol
rules.description:
Threat actors can use an older version of the auditpol binary available inside the NT resource kit to change audit policy configuration to impair detection capability.
This can be carried out by selectively disabling/removing certain audit policies as well as restoring a custom policy owned by the threat actor.

rules.title: Audit Policy Tampering Via Auditpol
rules.description:
Threat actors can use auditpol binary to change audit policy configuration to impair detection capability.
This can be carried out by selectively disabling/removing certain audit policies as well as restoring a custom policy owned by the threat actor.

rules.title: Indirect Inline Command Execution Via Bash.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of Microsoft bash launcher with the "-c" flag.
This can be used to potentially bypass defenses and execute Linux or Windows-based binaries directly via bash.

rules.title: Indirect Command Execution From Script File Via Bash.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of Microsoft bash launcher without any flags to execute the content of a bash script directly.
This can be used to potentially bypass defenses and execute Linux or Windows-based binaries directly via bash.

rules.title: Boot Configuration Tampering Via Bcdedit.EXE
rules.description: Detects the use of the bcdedit command to tamper with the boot configuration data. This technique is often times used by malware or attackers as a destructive way before launching ransomware.

rules.title: Potential Ransomware or Unauthorized MBR Tampering Via Bcdedit.EXE
rules.description: Detects potential malicious and unauthorized usage of bcdedit.exe

rules.title: Data Export From MSSQL Table Via BCP.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of the BCP utility in order to export data from the database.
Attackers were seen saving their malware to a database column or table and then later extracting it via "bcp.exe" into a file.

rules.title: Suspicious Child Process Of BgInfo.EXE
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes of "BgInfo.exe" which could be a sign of potential abuse of the binary to proxy execution via external VBScript

rules.title: Uncommon Child Process Of BgInfo.EXE
rules.description: Detects uncommon child processes of "BgInfo.exe" which could be a sign of potential abuse of the binary to proxy execution via external VBScript

rules.title: BitLockerTogo.EXE Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of "BitLockerToGo.EXE".
BitLocker To Go is BitLocker Drive Encryption on removable data drives. This feature includes the encryption of, USB flash drives, SD cards, External hard disk drives, Other drives that are formatted by using the NTFS, FAT16, FAT32, or exFAT file system.
This is a rarely used application and usage of it at all is worth investigating.
Malware such as Lumma stealer has been seen using this process as a target for process hollowing.

rules.title: File Download Via Bitsadmin
rules.description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file

rules.title: Suspicious Download From Direct IP Via Bitsadmin
rules.description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file using an URL that contains an IP

rules.title: Suspicious Download From File-Sharing Website Via Bitsadmin
rules.description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file from a suspicious domain

rules.title: File With Suspicious Extension Downloaded Via Bitsadmin
rules.description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file with a suspicious extension

rules.title: File Download Via Bitsadmin To A Suspicious Target Folder
rules.description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file to a suspicious target folder

rules.title: Monitoring For Persistence Via BITS
rules.description:
BITS will allow you to schedule a command to execute after a successful download to notify you that the job is finished.
When the job runs on the system the command specified in the BITS job will be executed.
This can be abused by actors to create a backdoor within the system and for persistence.
It will be chained in a BITS job to schedule the download of malware/additional binaries and execute the program after being downloaded.

rules.title: Potential Data Stealing Via Chromium Headless Debugging
rules.description: Detects chromium based browsers starting in headless and debugging mode and pointing to a user profile. This could be a sign of data stealing or remote control

rules.title: Browser Execution In Headless Mode
rules.description: Detects execution of Chromium based browser in headless mode

rules.title: File Download with Headless Browser
rules.description: Detects execution of chromium based browser in headless mode using the "dump-dom" command line to download files

rules.title: Chromium Browser Instance Executed With Custom Extension
rules.description: Detects a Chromium based browser process with the 'load-extension' flag to start a instance with a custom extension

rules.title: Chromium Browser Headless Execution To Mockbin Like Site
rules.description: Detects the execution of a Chromium based browser process with the "headless" flag and a URL pointing to the mockbin.org service (which can be used to exfiltrate data).

rules.title: Suspicious Chromium Browser Instance Executed With Custom Extension
rules.description: Detects a suspicious process spawning a Chromium based browser process with the 'load-extension' flag to start an instance with a custom extension

rules.title: File Download From Browser Process Via Inline URL
rules.description: Detects execution of a browser process with a URL argument pointing to a file with a potentially interesting extension. This can be abused to download arbitrary files or to hide from the user for example by launching the browser in a minimized state.

rules.title: Browser Started with Remote Debugging
rules.description: Detects browsers starting with the remote debugging flags. Which is a technique often used to perform browser injection attacks

rules.title: Tor Client/Browser Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of Tor or Tor-Browser to connect to onion routing networks

rules.title: Suspicious Calculator Usage
rules.description: Detects suspicious use of 'calc.exe' with command line parameters or in a suspicious directory, which is likely caused by some PoC or detection evasion.

rules.title: Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via Cdb.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of "cdb.exe" to launch arbitrary processes or commands from a debugger script file

rules.title: New Root Certificate Installed Via CertMgr.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of "certmgr" with the "add" flag in order to install a new certificate on the system.
Adversaries may install a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.

rules.title: File Download via CertOC.EXE
rules.description: Detects when a user downloads a file by using CertOC.exe

rules.title: File Download From IP Based URL Via CertOC.EXE
rules.description: Detects when a user downloads a file from an IP based URL using CertOC.exe

rules.title: DLL Loaded via CertOC.EXE
rules.description: Detects when a user installs certificates by using CertOC.exe to loads the target DLL file.

rules.title: Suspicious DLL Loaded via CertOC.EXE
rules.description: Detects when a user installs certificates by using CertOC.exe to load the target DLL file.

rules.title: New Root Certificate Installed Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of "certutil" with the "addstore" flag in order to install a new certificate on the system.
Adversaries may install a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.

rules.title: File Decoded From Base64/Hex Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of certutil with either the "decode" or "decodehex" flags to decode base64 or hex encoded files. This can be abused by attackers to decode an encoded payload before execution

rules.title: Suspicious Download Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of certutil with certain flags that allow the utility to download files.

rules.title: Suspicious File Downloaded From Direct IP Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of certutil with certain flags that allow the utility to download files from direct IPs.

rules.title: Suspicious File Downloaded From File-Sharing Website Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of certutil with certain flags that allow the utility to download files from file-sharing websites.

rules.title: File Encoded To Base64 Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of certutil with the "encode" flag to encode a file to base64. This can be abused by threat actors and attackers for data exfiltration

rules.title: Suspicious File Encoded To Base64 Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of certutil with the "encode" flag to encode a file to base64 where the extensions of the file is suspicious

rules.title: File In Suspicious Location Encoded To Base64 Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of certutil with the "encode" flag to encode a file to base64 where the files are located in potentially suspicious locations

rules.title: Certificate Exported Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of the certutil with the "exportPFX" flag which allows the utility to export certificates.

rules.title: Potential NTLM Coercion Via Certutil.EXE
rules.description: Detects possible NTLM coercion via certutil using the 'syncwithWU' flag

rules.title: Console CodePage Lookup Via CHCP
rules.description: Detects use of chcp to look up the system locale value as part of host discovery

rules.title: Suspicious CodePage Switch Via CHCP
rules.description: Detects a code page switch in command line or batch scripts to a rare language

rules.title: Deleted Data Overwritten Via Cipher.EXE
rules.description:
Detects usage of the "cipher" built-in utility in order to overwrite deleted data from disk.
Adversaries may destroy data and files on specific systems or in large numbers on a network to interrupt availability to systems, services, and network resources.
Data destruction is likely to render stored data irrecoverable by forensic techniques through overwriting files or data on local and remote drives

rules.title: Process Access via TrolleyExpress Exclusion
rules.description: Detects a possible process memory dump that uses the white-listed Citrix TrolleyExpress.exe filename as a way to dump the lsass process memory

rules.title: Data Copied To Clipboard Via Clip.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of clip.exe in order to copy data to the clipboard. Adversaries may collect data stored in the clipboard from users copying information within or between applications.

rules.title: Cloudflared Portable Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of the "cloudflared" binary from a non standard location.

rules.title: Cloudflared Quick Tunnel Execution
rules.description:
Detects creation of an ad-hoc Cloudflare Quick Tunnel, which can be used to tunnel local services such as HTTP, RDP, SSH and SMB.
The free TryCloudflare Quick Tunnel will generate a random subdomain on trycloudflare[.]com, following a call to api[.]trycloudflare[.]com.
The tool has been observed in use by threat groups including Akira ransomware.

rules.title: Cloudflared Tunnel Connections Cleanup
rules.description: Detects execution of the "cloudflared" tool with the tunnel "cleanup" flag in order to cleanup tunnel connections.

rules.title: Cloudflared Tunnel Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of the "cloudflared" tool to connect back to a tunnel. This was seen used by threat actors to maintain persistence and remote access to compromised networks.

rules.title: Change Default File Association Via Assoc
rules.description:
Detects file association changes using the builtin "assoc" command.
When a file is opened, the default program used to open the file (also called the file association or handler) is checked. File association selections are stored in the Windows Registry and can be edited by users, administrators, or programs that have Registry access or by administrators using the built-in assoc utility. Applications can modify the file association for a given file extension to call an arbitrary program when a file with the given extension is opened.

rules.title: Change Default File Association To Executable Via Assoc
rules.description:
Detects when a program changes the default file association of any extension to an executable.
When a file is opened, the default program used to open the file (also called the file association or handler) is checked. File association selections are stored in the Windows Registry and can be edited by users, administrators, or programs that have Registry access or by administrators using the built-in assoc utility. Applications can modify the file association for a given file extension to call an arbitrary program when a file with the given extension is opened.

rules.title: Copy .DMP/.DUMP Files From Remote Share Via Cmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of the copy builtin cmd command to copy files with the ".dmp"/".dump" extension from a remote share

rules.title: Curl Download And Execute Combination
rules.description: Adversaries can use curl to download payloads remotely and execute them. Curl is included by default in Windows 10 build 17063 and later.

rules.title: File Deletion Via Del
rules.description:
Detects execution of the builtin "del"/"erase" commands in order to delete files.
Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity.
Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how.
Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.

rules.title: Greedy File Deletion Using Del
rules.description: Detects execution of the "del" builtin command to remove files using greedy/wildcard expression. This is often used by malware to delete content of folders that perhaps contains the initial malware infection or to delete evidence.

rules.title: File And SubFolder Enumeration Via Dir Command
rules.description: Detects usage of the "dir" command part of Windows CMD with the "/S" command line flag in order to enumerate files in a specified directory and all subdirectories.

rules.title: Potential Dosfuscation Activity
rules.description: Detects possible payload obfuscation via the commandline

rules.title: Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings
rules.description: Detects a suspicious command line execution that includes an URL and AppData string in the command line parameters as used by several droppers (js/vbs > powershell)

rules.title: Potential Privilege Escalation Using Symlink Between Osk and Cmd
rules.description: Detects the creation of a symbolic link between "cmd.exe" and the accessibility on-screen keyboard binary (osk.exe) using "mklink". This technique provides an elevated command prompt to the user from the login screen without the need to log in.

rules.title: VolumeShadowCopy Symlink Creation Via Mklink
rules.description: Shadow Copies storage symbolic link creation using operating systems utilities

rules.title: Suspicious File Execution From Internet Hosted WebDav Share
rules.description: Detects the execution of the "net use" command to mount a WebDAV server and then immediately execute some content in it. As seen being used in malicious LNK files

rules.title: Cmd.EXE Missing Space Characters Execution Anomaly
rules.description:
Detects Windows command lines that miss a space before or after the /c flag when running a command using the cmd.exe.
This could be a sign of obfuscation of a fat finger problem (typo by the developer).

rules.title: NtdllPipe Like Activity Execution
rules.description: Detects command that type the content of ntdll.dll to a different file or a pipe in order to evade AV / EDR detection. As seen being used in the POC NtdllPipe

rules.title: Potential CommandLine Path Traversal Via Cmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects potential path traversal attempt via cmd.exe. Could indicate possible command/argument confusion/hijacking

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Ping/Copy Command Combination
rules.description: Detects uncommon and potentially suspicious one-liner command containing both "ping" and "copy" at the same time, which is usually used by malware.

rules.title: Suspicious Ping/Del Command Combination
rules.description: Detects a method often used by ransomware. Which combines the "ping" to wait a couple of seconds and then "del" to delete the file in question. Its used to hide the file responsible for the initial infection for example

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious CMD Shell Output Redirect
rules.description:
Detects inline Windows shell commands redirecting output via the ">" symbol to a suspicious location.
This technique is sometimes used by malicious actors in order to redirect the output of reconnaissance commands such as "hostname" and "dir" to files for future exfiltration.

rules.title: Directory Removal Via Rmdir
rules.description:
Detects execution of the builtin "rmdir" command in order to delete directories.
Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity.
Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how.
Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.

rules.title: Copy From VolumeShadowCopy Via Cmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of the builtin "copy" command that targets a shadow copy (sometimes used to copy registry hives that are in use)

rules.title: Read Contents From Stdin Via Cmd.EXE
rules.description: Detect the use of "<" to read and potentially execute a file via cmd.exe

rules.title: Sticky Key Like Backdoor Execution
rules.description: Detects the usage and installation of a backdoor that uses an option to register a malicious debugger for built-in tools that are accessible in the login screen

rules.title: Persistence Via Sticky Key Backdoor
rules.description:
By replacing the sticky keys executable with the local admins CMD executable, an attacker is able to access a privileged windows console session without authenticating to the system.
When the sticky keys are "activated" the privilleged shell is launched.

rules.title: Potential Download/Upload Activity Using Type Command
rules.description: Detects usage of the "type" command to download/upload data from WebDAV server

rules.title: Unusual Parent Process For Cmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects suspicious parent process for cmd.exe

rules.title: New Generic Credentials Added Via Cmdkey.EXE
rules.description:
Detects usage of "cmdkey.exe" to add generic credentials.
As an example, this can be used before connecting to an RDP session via command line interface.

rules.title: Potential Reconnaissance For Cached Credentials Via Cmdkey.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of cmdkey to look for cached credentials on the system

rules.title: Potential Arbitrary File Download Via Cmdl32.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of Cmdl32 with the "/vpn" and "/lan" flags.
Attackers can abuse this utility in order to download arbitrary files via a configuration file.
Inspect the location and the content of the file passed as an argument in order to determine if it is suspicious.

rules.title: CMSTP Execution Process Creation
rules.description: Detects various indicators of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer execution

rules.title: Arbitrary File Download Via ConfigSecurityPolicy.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of "ConfigSecurityPolicy.EXE", a binary part of Windows Defender used to manage settings in Windows Defender.
Users can configure different pilot collections for each of the co-management workloads.
It can be abused by attackers in order to upload or download files.

rules.title: Powershell Executed From Headless ConHost Process
rules.description:
Detects the use of powershell commands from headless ConHost window.
The "--headless" flag hides the windows from the user upon execution.

rules.title: Suspicious High IntegrityLevel Conhost Legacy Option
rules.description: ForceV1 asks for information directly from the kernel space. Conhost connects to the console application. High IntegrityLevel means the process is running with elevated privileges, such as an Administrator context.

rules.title: Conhost.exe CommandLine Path Traversal
rules.description: detects the usage of path traversal in conhost.exe indicating possible command/argument confusion/hijacking

rules.title: Uncommon Child Process Of Conhost.EXE
rules.description: Detects uncommon "conhost" child processes. This could be a sign of "conhost" usage as a LOLBIN or potential process injection activity.

rules.title: Conhost Spawned By Uncommon Parent Process
rules.description: Detects when the Console Window Host (conhost.exe) process is spawned by an uncommon parent process, which could be indicative of potential code injection activity.

rules.title: Control Panel Items
rules.description: Detects the malicious use of a control panel item

rules.title: CreateDump Process Dump
rules.description: Detects uses of the createdump.exe LOLOBIN utility to dump process memory

rules.title: Dynamic .NET Compilation Via Csc.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "csc.exe" to compile .NET code. Attackers often leverage this to compile code on the fly and use it in other stages.

rules.title: Csc.EXE Execution Form Potentially Suspicious Parent
rules.description: Detects a potentially suspicious parent of "csc.exe", which could be a sign of payload delivery.

rules.title: Suspicious Csi.exe Usage
rules.description: Csi.exe is a signed binary from Microsoft that comes with Visual Studio and provides C# interactive capabilities. It can be used to run C# code from a file passed as a parameter in command line. Early version of this utility provided with Microsoft “Roslyn” Community Technology Preview was named 'rcsi.exe'

rules.title: Suspicious Use of CSharp Interactive Console
rules.description: Detects the execution of CSharp interactive console by PowerShell

rules.title: Active Directory Structure Export Via Csvde.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of "csvde.exe" in order to export organizational Active Directory structure.

rules.title: Potential Cookies Session Hijacking
rules.description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "-c" flag in order to save cookie data.

rules.title: Curl Web Request With Potential Custom User-Agent
rules.description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with a potential custom "User-Agent". Attackers can leverage this to download or exfiltrate data via "curl" to a domain that only accept specific "User-Agent" strings

rules.title: File Download From IP URL Via Curl.EXE
rules.description: Detects file downloads directly from IP address URL using curl.exe

rules.title: Suspicious File Download From IP Via Curl.EXE
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious file downloads directly from IP addresses using curl.exe

rules.title: Suspicious File Download From File Sharing Domain Via Curl.EXE
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious file download from file sharing domains using curl.exe

rules.title: Insecure Transfer Via Curl.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "--insecure" flag.

rules.title: Insecure Proxy/DOH Transfer Via Curl.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "insecure" flag over proxy or DOH.

rules.title: Local File Read Using Curl.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "file://" protocol handler in order to read local files.

rules.title: Suspicious Curl.EXE Download
rules.description: Detects a suspicious curl process start on Windows and outputs the requested document to a local file

rules.title: Suspicious CustomShellHost Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of CustomShellHost.exe where the child isn't located in 'C:\Windows\explorer.exe'. CustomShellHost is a known LOLBin that can be abused by attackers for defense evasion techniques.

rules.title: ManageEngine Endpoint Central Dctask64.EXE Potential Abuse
rules.description:
Detects the execution of "dctask64.exe", a signed binary by ZOHO Corporation part of ManageEngine Endpoint Central.
This binary can be abused for DLL injection, arbitrary command and process execution.

rules.title: Uncommon Child Process Of Defaultpack.EXE
rules.description: Detects uncommon child processes of "DefaultPack.EXE" binary as a proxy to launch other programs

rules.title: Remote File Download Via Desktopimgdownldr Utility
rules.description: Detects the desktopimgdownldr utility being used to download a remote file. An adversary may use desktopimgdownldr to download arbitrary files as an alternative to certutil.

rules.title: Suspicious Desktopimgdownldr Command
rules.description: Detects a suspicious Microsoft desktopimgdownldr execution with parameters used to download files from the Internet

rules.title: DeviceCredentialDeployment Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of DeviceCredentialDeployment to hide a process from view.

rules.title: Potential DLL Sideloading Via DeviceEnroller.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the use of the PhoneDeepLink parameter to potentially sideload a DLL file that does not exist. This non-existent DLL file is named "ShellChromeAPI.dll".
Adversaries can drop their own renamed DLL and execute it via DeviceEnroller.exe using this parameter

rules.title: Arbitrary MSI Download Via Devinit.EXE
rules.description: Detects a certain command line flag combination used by "devinit.exe", which can be abused as a LOLBIN to download arbitrary MSI packages on a Windows system

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of ClickOnce Application
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of a ClickOnce deployment application

rules.title: DirLister Execution
rules.description: Detect the usage of "DirLister.exe" a utility for quickly listing folder or drive contents. It was seen used by BlackCat ransomware to create a list of accessible directories and files.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of DiskShadow.EXE
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "Diskshadow.exe". This could be an attempt to bypass parent/child relationship detection or application whitelisting rules.

rules.title: Diskshadow Script Mode - Uncommon Script Extension Execution
rules.description:
Detects execution of "Diskshadow.exe" in script mode to execute an script with a potentially uncommon extension.
Initial baselining of the allowed extension list is required.

rules.title: Diskshadow Script Mode - Execution From Potential Suspicious Location
rules.description: Detects execution of "Diskshadow.exe" in script mode using the "/s" flag where the script is located in a potentially suspicious location.

rules.title: PowerShell Web Access Feature Enabled Via DISM
rules.description: Detects the use of DISM to enable the PowerShell Web Access feature, which could be used for remote access and potential abuse

rules.title: Dism Remove Online Package
rules.description: Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool. DISM is used to enumerate, install, uninstall, configure, and update features and packages in Windows images

rules.title: DLL Sideloading by VMware Xfer Utility
rules.description: Detects execution of VMware Xfer utility (VMwareXferlogs.exe) from the non-default directory which may be an attempt to sideload arbitrary DLL

rules.title: Dllhost.EXE Execution Anomaly
rules.description: Detects a "dllhost" process spawning with no commandline arguments which is very rare to happen and could indicate process injection activity or malware mimicking similar system processes.

rules.title: DNS Exfiltration and Tunneling Tools Execution
rules.description: Well-known DNS Exfiltration tools execution

rules.title: Unusual Child Process of dns.exe
rules.description: Detects an unexpected process spawning from dns.exe which may indicate activity related to remote code execution or other forms of exploitation as seen in CVE-2020-1350 (SigRed)

rules.title: Potential Discovery Activity Via Dnscmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects an attempt to leverage dnscmd.exe to enumerate the DNS zones of a domain. DNS zones used to host the DNS records for a particular domain.

rules.title: New DNS ServerLevelPluginDll Installed Via Dnscmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects the installation of a DNS plugin DLL via ServerLevelPluginDll parameter in registry, which can be used to execute code in context of the DNS server (restart required)

rules.title: Potential Application Whitelisting Bypass via Dnx.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of Dnx.EXE. The Dnx utility allows for the execution of C# code.
Attackers might abuse this in order to bypass application whitelisting.

rules.title: Arbitrary DLL or Csproj Code Execution Via Dotnet.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of arbitrary DLLs or unsigned code via a ".csproj" files via Dotnet.EXE.

rules.title: Binary Proxy Execution Via Dotnet-Trace.EXE
rules.description: Detects commandline arguments for executing a child process via dotnet-trace.exe

rules.title: Process Memory Dump Via Dotnet-Dump
rules.description: Detects the execution of "dotnet-dump" with the "collect" flag. The execution could indicate potential process dumping of critical processes such as LSASS.

rules.title: Potential Recon Activity Using DriverQuery.EXE
rules.description: Detect usage of the "driverquery" utility to perform reconnaissance on installed drivers

rules.title: DriverQuery.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detect usage of the "driverquery" utility. Which can be used to perform reconnaissance on installed drivers

rules.title: Potentially Over Permissive Permissions Granted Using Dsacls.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of Dsacls to grant over permissive permissions

rules.title: Potential Password Spraying Attempt Using Dsacls.EXE
rules.description: Detects possible password spraying attempts using Dsacls

rules.title: Domain Trust Discovery Via Dsquery
rules.description: Detects execution of "dsquery.exe" for domain trust discovery

rules.title: Suspicious Kernel Dump Using Dtrace
rules.description: Detects suspicious way to dump the kernel on Windows systems using dtrace.exe, which is available on Windows systems since Windows 10 19H1

rules.title: Potential Windows Defender AV Bypass Via Dump64.EXE Rename
rules.description:
Detects when a user is potentially trying to bypass the Windows Defender AV by renaming a tool to dump64.exe and placing it in the Visual Studio folder.
Currently the rule is covering only usage of procdump but other utilities can be added in order to increase coverage.

rules.title: DumpMinitool Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of "DumpMinitool.exe" a tool that allows the dump of process memory via the use of the "MiniDumpWriteDump"

rules.title: Suspicious DumpMinitool Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious ways to use the "DumpMinitool.exe" binary

rules.title: New Capture Session Launched Via DXCap.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of "DXCap.EXE" with the "-c" flag, which allows a user to launch any arbitrary binary or windows package through DXCap itself. This can be abused to potentially bypass application whitelisting.

rules.title: Esentutl Gather Credentials
rules.description: Conti recommendation to its affiliates to use esentutl to access NTDS dumped file. Trickbot also uses this utilities to get MSEdge info via its module pwgrab.

rules.title: Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data
rules.description: Files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) copying

rules.title: Esentutl Steals Browser Information
rules.description: One way Qbot steals sensitive information is by extracting browser data from Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge by using the built-in utility esentutl.exe

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Event Viewer Child Process
rules.description: Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes of "eventvwr.exe" which might indicate a UAC bypass attempt

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion
rules.description: Detects the expansion or decompression of cabinet files from potentially suspicious or uncommon locations, e.g. seen in Iranian MeteorExpress related attacks

rules.title: Explorer Process Tree Break
rules.description:
Detects a command line process that uses explorer.exe to launch arbitrary commands or binaries,
which is similar to cmd.exe /c, only it breaks the process tree and makes its parent a new instance of explorer spawning from "svchost"

rules.title: File Explorer Folder Opened Using Explorer Folder Shortcut Via Shell
rules.description: Detects the initial execution of "cmd.exe" which spawns "explorer.exe" with the appropriate command line arguments for opening the "My Computer" folder.

rules.title: Explorer NOUACCHECK Flag
rules.description: Detects suspicious starts of explorer.exe that use the /NOUACCHECK flag that allows to run all sub processes of that newly started explorer.exe without any UAC checks

rules.title: Remote File Download Via Findstr.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "findstr" with specific flags and a remote share path. This specific set of CLI flags would allow "findstr" to download the content of the file located on the remote share as described in the LOLBAS entry.

rules.title: Findstr GPP Passwords
rules.description: Look for the encrypted cpassword value within Group Policy Preference files on the Domain Controller. This value can be decrypted with gpp-decrypt.

rules.title: Findstr Launching .lnk File
rules.description: Detects usage of findstr to identify and execute a lnk file as seen within the HHS redirect attack

rules.title: LSASS Process Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
rules.description: Detects findstring commands that include the keyword lsass, which indicates recon actviity for the LSASS process PID

rules.title: Permission Misconfiguration Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
rules.description:
Detects usage of findstr with the "EVERYONE" or "BUILTIN" keywords.
This was seen being used in combination with "icacls" and other utilities to spot misconfigured files or folders permissions.

rules.title: Recon Command Output Piped To Findstr.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of a potential recon command where the results are piped to "findstr". This is meant to trigger on inline calls of "cmd.exe" via the "/c" or "/k" for example.
Attackers often time use this technique to extract specific information they require in their reconnaissance phase.

rules.title: Security Tools Keyword Lookup Via Findstr.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of "findstr" to search for common names of security tools. Attackers often pipe the results of recon commands such as "tasklist" or "whoami" to "findstr" in order to filter out the results.
This detection focuses on the keywords that the attacker might use as a filter.

rules.title: Insensitive Subfolder Search Via Findstr.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of findstr with the "s" and "i" flags for a "subfolder" and "insensitive" search respectively. Attackers sometimes leverage this built-in utility to search the system for interesting files or filter through results of commands.

rules.title: Sysmon Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude Using Findstr.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of "findstr" with the argument "385201". Which could indicate potential discovery of an installed Sysinternals Sysmon service using the default driver altitude (even if the name is changed).

rules.title: Finger.EXE Execution
rules.description:
Detects execution of the "finger.exe" utility.
Finger.EXE or "TCPIP Finger Command" is an old utility that is still present on modern Windows installation. It Displays information about users on a specified remote computer (typically a UNIX computer) that is running the finger service or daemon.
Due to the old nature of this utility and the rareness of machines having the finger service. Any execution of "finger.exe" can be considered "suspicious" and worth investigating.

rules.title: Filter Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
rules.description: Detect filter driver unloading activity via fltmc.exe

rules.title: Sysmon Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
rules.description: Detects possible Sysmon filter driver unloaded via fltmc.exe

rules.title: Forfiles.EXE Child Process Masquerading
rules.description: Detects the execution of "forfiles" from a non-default location, in order to potentially spawn a custom "cmd.exe" from the current working directory.

rules.title: Forfiles Command Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of "forfiles" with the "/c" flag.
While this is an expected behavior of the tool, it can be abused in order to proxy execution through it with any binary.
Can be used to bypass application whitelisting.

rules.title: Uncommon FileSystem Load Attempt By Format.com
rules.description: Detects the execution of format.com with an uncommon filesystem selection that could indicate a defense evasion activity in which "format.com" is used to load malicious DLL files or other programs.

rules.title: Use of FSharp Interpreters
rules.description:
Detects the execution of FSharp Interpreters "FsiAnyCpu.exe" and "FSi.exe"
Both can be used for AWL bypass and to execute F# code via scripts or inline.

rules.title: Fsutil Drive Enumeration
rules.description: Attackers may leverage fsutil to enumerated connected drives.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious NTFS Symlink Behavior Modification
rules.description: Detects the modification of NTFS symbolic link behavior using fsutil, which could be used to enable remote to local or remote to remote symlinks for potential attacks.

rules.title: Fsutil Suspicious Invocation
rules.description:
Detects suspicious parameters of fsutil (deleting USN journal, configuring it with small size, etc).
Might be used by ransomwares during the attack (seen by NotPetya and others).

rules.title: Potential Arbitrary Command Execution Via FTP.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "ftp.exe" script with the "-s" or "/s" flag and any child processes ran by "ftp.exe".

rules.title: Arbitrary File Download Via GfxDownloadWrapper.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of GfxDownloadWrapper.exe with a URL as an argument to download file.

rules.title: Suspicious Git Clone
rules.description: Detects execution of "git" in order to clone a remote repository that contain suspicious keywords which might be suspicious

rules.title: Github Self-Hosted Runner Execution
rules.description:
Detects GitHub self-hosted runners executing workflows on local infrastructure that could be abused for persistence and code execution.
Shai-Hulud is an npm supply chain worm targeting CI/CD environments.
It installs runners on compromised systems to maintain access after credential theft, leveraging their access to secrets and internal networks.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious GoogleUpdate Child Process
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "GoogleUpdate.exe"

rules.title: File Decryption Using Gpg4win
rules.description: Detects usage of Gpg4win to decrypt files

rules.title: File Encryption Using Gpg4win
rules.description: Detects usage of Gpg4win to encrypt files

rules.title: Portable Gpg.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of "gpg.exe" from uncommon location. Often used by ransomware and loaders to decrypt/encrypt data.

rules.title: File Encryption/Decryption Via Gpg4win From Suspicious Locations
rules.description: Detects usage of Gpg4win to encrypt/decrypt files located in potentially suspicious locations.

rules.title: Gpresult Display Group Policy Information
rules.description: Detects cases in which a user uses the built-in Windows utility gpresult to display the Resultant Set of Policy (RSoP) information

rules.title: Arbitrary Binary Execution Using GUP Utility
rules.description: Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater (gup) to launch other commands or executables

rules.title: File Download Using Notepad++ GUP Utility
rules.description: Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater (gup) from a process other than Notepad++ to download files.

rules.title: Suspicious GUP Usage
rules.description: Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater in a suspicious directory, which is often used in DLL side-loading attacks

rules.title: HH.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of "hh.exe" to open ".chm" files.

rules.title: Remote CHM File Download/Execution Via HH.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of "hh.exe" to execute/download remotely hosted ".chm" files.

rules.title: HTML Help HH.EXE Suspicious Child Process
rules.description: Detects a suspicious child process of a Microsoft HTML Help (HH.exe)

rules.title: Suspicious HH.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects a suspicious execution of a Microsoft HTML Help (HH.exe)

rules.title: HackTool - ADCSPwn Execution
rules.description: Detects command line parameters used by ADCSPwn, a tool to escalate privileges in an active directory network by coercing authenticate from machine accounts and relaying to the certificate service

rules.title: HackTool - Bloodhound/Sharphound Execution
rules.description: Detects command line parameters used by Bloodhound and Sharphound hack tools

rules.title: HackTool - F-Secure C3 Load by Rundll32
rules.description: F-Secure C3 produces DLLs with a default exported StartNodeRelay function.

rules.title: HackTool - Certify Execution
rules.description: Detects Certify a tool for Active Directory certificate abuse based on PE metadata characteristics and common command line arguments.

rules.title: HackTool - Certipy Execution
rules.description: Detects Certipy execution, a tool for Active Directory Certificate Services enumeration and abuse based on PE metadata characteristics and common command line arguments.

rules.title: Operator Bloopers Cobalt Strike Commands
rules.description: Detects use of Cobalt Strike commands accidentally entered in the CMD shell

rules.title: Operator Bloopers Cobalt Strike Modules
rules.description: Detects Cobalt Strike module/commands accidentally entered in CMD shell

rules.title: CobaltStrike Load by Rundll32
rules.description: Rundll32 can be use by Cobalt Strike with StartW function to load DLLs from the command line.

rules.title: Potential CobaltStrike Process Patterns
rules.description: Detects potential process patterns related to Cobalt Strike beacon activity

rules.title: HackTool - CoercedPotato Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of CoercedPotato, a tool for privilege escalation

rules.title: HackTool - Covenant PowerShell Launcher
rules.description: Detects suspicious command lines used in Covenant luanchers

rules.title: HackTool - CrackMapExec Execution
rules.description: This rule detect common flag combinations used by CrackMapExec in order to detect its use even if the binary has been replaced.

rules.title: HackTool - CrackMapExec Execution Patterns
rules.description: Detects various execution patterns of the CrackMapExec pentesting framework

rules.title: HackTool - CrackMapExec Process Patterns
rules.description: Detects suspicious process patterns found in logs when CrackMapExec is used

rules.title: HackTool - CrackMapExec PowerShell Obfuscation
rules.description: The CrachMapExec pentesting framework implements a PowerShell obfuscation with some static strings detected by this rule.

rules.title: HackTool - CreateMiniDump Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of CreateMiniDump hack tool used to dump the LSASS process memory for credential extraction on the attacker's machine

rules.title: HackTool - DInjector PowerShell Cradle Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of the Dinject PowerShell cradle based on the specific flags

rules.title: HackTool - Dumpert Process Dumper Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of Dumpert process dumper, which dumps the lsass.exe process memory

rules.title: HackTool - EDRSilencer Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of EDRSilencer, a tool that leverages Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) to block Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) agents from reporting security events to the server based on PE metadata information.

rules.title: HackTool - Empire PowerShell Launch Parameters
rules.description: Detects suspicious powershell command line parameters used in Empire

rules.title: HackTool - Empire PowerShell UAC Bypass
rules.description: Detects some Empire PowerShell UAC bypass methods

rules.title: HackTool - WinRM Access Via Evil-WinRM
rules.description: Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to log into a computer using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.

rules.title: Hacktool Execution - Imphash
rules.description: Detects the execution of different Windows based hacktools via their import hash (imphash) even if the files have been renamed

rules.title: Hacktool Execution - PE Metadata
rules.description: Detects the execution of different Windows based hacktools via PE metadata (company, product, etc.) even if the files have been renamed

rules.title: HackTool - GMER Rootkit Detector and Remover Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution GMER tool based on image and hash fields.

rules.title: HackTool - HandleKatz LSASS Dumper Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of HandleKatz, a tool that demonstrates the usage of cloned handles to Lsass in order to create an obfuscated memory dump of the same

rules.title: HackTool - Hashcat Password Cracker Execution
rules.description: Execute Hashcat.exe with provided SAM file from registry of Windows and Password list to crack against

rules.title: HackTool - Htran/NATBypass Execution
rules.description: Detects executable names or flags used by Htran or Htran-like tools (e.g. NATBypass)

rules.title: HackTool - Hydra Password Bruteforce Execution
rules.description: Detects command line parameters used by Hydra password guessing hack tool

rules.title: HackTool - Potential Impacket Lateral Movement Activity
rules.description: Detects wmiexec/dcomexec/atexec/smbexec from Impacket framework

rules.title: HackTool - Impacket Tools Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of different compiled Windows binaries of the impacket toolset (based on names or part of their names - could lead to false positives)

rules.title: HackTool - Impersonate Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of the Impersonate tool. Which can be used to manipulate tokens on a Windows computers remotely (PsExec/WmiExec) or interactively

rules.title: HackTool - Inveigh Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of Inveigh a cross-platform .NET IPv4/IPv6 machine-in-the-middle tool

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation CLIP+ Launcher
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated use of Clip.exe to execute PowerShell

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation Obfuscated IEX Invocation
rules.description: Detects all variations of obfuscated powershell IEX invocation code generated by Invoke-Obfuscation framework from the following code block

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation STDIN+ Launcher
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated use of stdin to execute PowerShell

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation VAR+ Launcher
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated use of Environment Variables to execute PowerShell

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation COMPRESS OBFUSCATION
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via COMPRESS OBFUSCATION

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation Via Stdin
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via Stdin in Scripts

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Clip
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Clip.exe in Scripts

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use MSHTA in Scripts

rules.title: Invoke-Obfuscation VAR++ LAUNCHER OBFUSCATION
rules.description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via VAR++ LAUNCHER

rules.title: HackTool - Jlaive In-Memory Assembly Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of Jlaive to execute assemblies in a copied PowerShell

rules.title: HackTool - Koadic Execution
rules.description: Detects command line parameters used by Koadic hack tool

rules.title: HackTool - KrbRelay Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of KrbRelay, a Kerberos relaying tool

rules.title: HackTool - RemoteKrbRelay Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of RemoteKrbRelay, a Kerberos relaying tool via CommandLine flags and PE metadata.

rules.title: HackTool - KrbRelayUp Execution
rules.description: Detects KrbRelayUp used to perform a universal no-fix local privilege escalation in Windows domain environments where LDAP signing is not enforced

rules.title: HackTool - LocalPotato Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of the LocalPotato POC based on basic PE metadata information and default CLI examples

rules.title: Potential Meterpreter/CobaltStrike Activity
rules.description: Detects the use of getsystem Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command by detecting a specific service starting

rules.title: HackTool - Mimikatz Execution
rules.description: Detection well-known mimikatz command line arguments

rules.title: HackTool - PCHunter Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious use of PCHunter, a tool like Process Hacker to view and manipulate processes, kernel options and other low level stuff

rules.title: HackTool - Default PowerSploit/Empire Scheduled Task Creation
rules.description: Detects the creation of a schtask via PowerSploit or Empire Default Configuration.

rules.title: HackTool - PowerTool Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of the tool PowerTool which has the ability to kill a process, delete its process file, unload drivers, and delete the driver files

rules.title: HackTool - PurpleSharp Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of the PurpleSharp adversary simulation tool

rules.title: HackTool - Pypykatz Credentials Dumping Activity
rules.description: Detects the usage of "pypykatz" to obtain stored credentials. Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the Security Account Manager (SAM) database through Windows registry where the SAM database is stored

rules.title: HackTool - Quarks PwDump Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of the Quarks PwDump tool via commandline arguments

rules.title: HackTool - RedMimicry Winnti Playbook Execution
rules.description: Detects actions caused by the RedMimicry Winnti playbook a automated breach emulations utility

rules.title: Potential SMB Relay Attack Tool Execution
rules.description: Detects different hacktools used for relay attacks on Windows for privilege escalation

rules.title: HackTool - Rubeus Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of the hacktool Rubeus via PE information of command line parameters

rules.title: HackTool - SafetyKatz Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of the hacktool SafetyKatz via PE information and default Image name

rules.title: HackTool - SecurityXploded Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of SecurityXploded Tools

rules.title: HackTool - PPID Spoofing SelectMyParent Tool Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of parent process ID spoofing tools like Didier Stevens tool SelectMyParent

rules.title: HackTool - SharpChisel Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of the Sharp Chisel via the commandline arguments

rules.title: HackTool - SharpDPAPI Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of the SharpDPAPI tool based on CommandLine flags and PE metadata.

Any instances of hxtsr.exe not in this folder may be malware camouflaging itself as HxTsr.exe

rules.title: Uncommon Link.EXE Parent Process
rules.description:
Detects an uncommon parent process of "LINK.EXE".
Link.EXE in Microsoft incremental linker. Its a utility usually bundled with Visual Studio installation.
Multiple utilities often found in the same folder (editbin.exe, dumpbin.exe, lib.exe, etc) have a hardcode call to the "LINK.EXE" binary without checking its validity.
This would allow an attacker to sideload any binary with the name "link.exe" if one of the aforementioned tools get executed from a different location.
By filtering the known locations of such utilities we can spot uncommon parent process of LINK.EXE that might be suspicious or malicious.

rules.title: Rebuild Performance Counter Values Via Lodctr.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of "lodctr.exe" to rebuild the performance counter registry values. This can be abused by attackers by providing a malicious config file to overwrite performance counter configuration to confuse and evade monitoring and security solutions.

Attackers abuse this utility to install malicious MOF scripts

Script blocks are not logged in this case, so this utility helps to bypass protection mechanisms based on the analysis of these logs.

This rule detects execution of a PowerShell code through the sqltoolsps.exe utility, which is included in the standard set of utilities supplied with the Microsoft SQL Server Management studio.
Script blocks are not logged in this case, so this utility helps to bypass protection mechanisms based on the analysis of these logs.
