rules.title: New Firewall Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects the addition of a new rule to the Windows firewall via netsh

values: advfirewall firewall add rule name=Dropbox dir=in action=allow "program=?:\Program Files (x86)\Dropbox\Client\Dropbox.exe" enable=yes profile=Any
advfirewall firewall add rule name=Dropbox dir=in action=allow "program=?:\Program Files\Dropbox\Client\Dropbox.exe" enable=yes profile=Any

rules.title: Suspicious Program Location Whitelisted In Firewall Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects Netsh command execution that whitelists a program located in a suspicious location in the Windows Firewall

rules.title: RDP Connection Allowed Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of the netsh command to open and allow connections to port 3389 (RDP). As seen used by Sarwent Malware

rules.title: Firewall Rule Deleted Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects the removal of a port or application rule in the Windows Firewall configuration using netsh

values: advfirewall firewall delete rule name="Avast Antivirus Admin Client"

rules.title: Firewall Disabled via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects netsh commands that turns off the Windows firewall

rules.title: Netsh Allow Group Policy on Microsoft Defender Firewall
rules.description: Adversaries may modify system firewalls in order to bypass controls limiting network usage

rules.title: Firewall Configuration Discovery Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Adversaries may look for details about the network configuration and settings of systems they access or through information discovery of remote systems

rules.title: Firewall Rule Update Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of netsh with the "advfirewall" and the "set" option in order to set new values for properties of a existing rule

rules.title: Potential Persistence Via Netsh Helper DLL
rules.description: Detects the execution of netsh with "add helper" flag in order to add a custom helper DLL. This technique can be abused to add a malicious helper DLL that can be used as a persistence proxy that gets called when netsh.exe is executed.

rules.title: New Network Trace Capture Started Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of netsh with the "trace" flag in order to start a network capture

rules.title: New Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of netsh commands that configure a new port forwarding (PortProxy) rule

rules.title: RDP Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of netsh to configure a port forwarding of port 3389 (RDP) rule

rules.title: Harvesting Of Wifi Credentials Via Netsh.EXE
rules.description: Detect the harvesting of wifi credentials using netsh.exe

rules.title: Nltest.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects nltest commands that can be used for information discovery

rules.title: Potential Recon Activity Via Nltest.EXE
rules.description: Detects nltest commands that can be used for information discovery

rules.title: Potential Arbitrary Code Execution Via Node.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution node.exe which is shipped with multiple software such as VMware, Adobe...etc. In order to execute arbitrary code. For example to establish reverse shell as seen in Log4j attacks...etc

rules.title: Node Process Executions
rules.description: Detects the execution of other scripts using the Node executable packaged with Adobe Creative Cloud

rules.title: Network Reconnaissance Activity
rules.description: Detects a set of suspicious network related commands often used in recon stages

rules.title: Nslookup PowerShell Download Cradle - ProcessCreation
rules.description: Detects suspicious powershell download cradle using nslookup. This cradle uses nslookup to extract payloads from DNS records

rules.title: Suspicious Usage Of Active Directory Diagnostic Tool (ntdsutil.exe)
rules.description: Detects execution of ntdsutil.exe to perform different actions such as restoring snapshots...etc.

rules.title: Invocation of Active Directory Diagnostic Tool (ntdsutil.exe)
rules.description: Detects execution of ntdsutil.exe, which can be used for various attacks against the NTDS database (NTDS.DIT)

rules.title: Driver/DLL Installation Via Odbcconf.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "odbcconf" with "INSTALLDRIVER" which installs a new ODBC driver. Attackers abuse this to install and run malicious DLLs.

rules.title: Suspicious Driver/DLL Installation Via Odbcconf.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "INSTALLDRIVER" action where the driver doesn't contain a ".dll" extension. This is often used as a defense evasion method.

rules.title: Odbcconf.EXE Suspicious DLL Location
rules.description: Detects execution of "odbcconf" where the path of the DLL being registered is located in a potentially suspicious location.

rules.title: New DLL Registered Via Odbcconf.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "odbcconf" with "REGSVR" in order to register a new DLL (equivalent to running regsvr32). Attackers abuse this to install and run malicious DLLs.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious DLL Registered Via Odbcconf.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "REGSVR" action where the DLL in question doesn't contain a ".dll" extension. Which is often used as a method to evade defenses.

rules.title: Response File Execution Via Odbcconf.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "-f" flag in order to load a response file which might contain a malicious action.

rules.title: Suspicious Response File Execution Via Odbcconf.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "-f" flag in order to load a response file with a non-".rsp" extension.

rules.title: Uncommon Child Process Spawned By Odbcconf.EXE
rules.description: Detects an uncommon child process of "odbcconf.exe" binary which normally shouldn't have any child processes.

rules.title: Potential Arbitrary File Download Using Office Application
rules.description: Detects potential arbitrary file download using a Microsoft Office application

rules.title: Potential Excel.EXE DCOM Lateral Movement Via ActivateMicrosoftApp
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes of Excel which could be an indicator of lateral movement leveraging the "ActivateMicrosoftApp" Excel DCOM object.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Office Document Executed From Trusted Location
rules.description: Detects the execution of an Office application that points to a document that is located in a trusted location. Attackers often used this to avoid macro security and execute their malicious code.

rules.title: OneNote.EXE Execution of Malicious Embedded Scripts
rules.description:
Detects the execution of malicious OneNote documents that contain embedded scripts.
When a user clicks on a OneNote attachment and then on the malicious link inside the ".one" file, it exports and executes the malicious embedded script from specific directories.

rules.title: Suspicious Microsoft OneNote Child Process
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes of the Microsoft OneNote application. This may indicate an attempt to execute malicious embedded objects from a .one file.

rules.title: Outlook EnableUnsafeClientMailRules Setting Enabled
rules.description: Detects an attacker trying to enable the outlook security setting "EnableUnsafeClientMailRules" which allows outlook to run applications or execute macros

rules.title: Suspicious Execution From Outlook Temporary Folder
rules.description: Detects a suspicious program execution in Outlook temp folder

rules.title: Suspicious Outlook Child Process
rules.description: Detects a suspicious process spawning from an Outlook process.

rules.title: Suspicious Remote Child Process From Outlook
rules.description: Detects a suspicious child process spawning from Outlook where the image is located in a remote location (SMB/WebDav shares).

rules.title: Suspicious Binary In User Directory Spawned From Office Application
rules.description: Detects an executable in the users directory started from one of the Microsoft Office suite applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Publisher, Visio)

rules.title: Suspicious Microsoft Office Child Process
rules.description: Detects a suspicious process spawning from one of the Microsoft Office suite products (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Publisher, Visio, etc.)

rules.title: Potential Arbitrary DLL Load Using Winword
rules.description: Detects potential DLL sideloading using the Microsoft Office winword process via the '/l' flag.

rules.title: Potential Mpclient.DLL Sideloading Via OfflineScannerShell.EXE Execution
rules.description:
Detects execution of Windows Defender "OfflineScannerShell.exe" from its non standard directory.
The "OfflineScannerShell.exe" binary is vulnerable to DLL side loading and will load any DLL named "mpclient.dll" from the current working directory.

rules.title: PDQ Deploy Remote Adminstartion Tool Execution
rules.description: Detect use of PDQ Deploy remote admin tool

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Execution Of PDQDeployRunner
rules.description: Detects suspicious execution of "PDQDeployRunner" which is part of the PDQDeploy service stack that is responsible for executing commands and packages on a remote machines

rules.title: Perl Inline Command Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of perl using the "-e"/"-E" flags. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live perl code.

rules.title: Php Inline Command Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of php using the "-r" flag. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live php code.

rules.title: Ping Hex IP
rules.description: Detects a ping command that uses a hex encoded IP address

rules.title: PktMon.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of PktMon, a tool that captures network packets.

rules.title: Suspicious Plink Port Forwarding
rules.description: Detects suspicious Plink tunnel port forwarding to a local port

values: Command-line SSH, Telnet, and Rlogin client

rules.title: Potential RDP Tunneling Via Plink
rules.description: Execution of plink to perform data exfiltration and tunneling

rules.title: Suspicious Powercfg Execution To Change Lock Screen Timeout
rules.description: Detects suspicious execution of 'Powercfg.exe' to change lock screen timeout

rules.title: AADInternals PowerShell Cmdlets Execution - ProccessCreation
rules.description: Detects ADDInternals Cmdlet execution. A tool for administering Azure AD and Office 365. Which can be abused by threat actors to attack Azure AD or Office 365.

rules.title: Potential Active Directory Enumeration Using AD Module - ProcCreation
rules.description: Detects usage of the "Import-Module" cmdlet to load the "Microsoft.ActiveDirectory.Management.dl" DLL. Which is often used by attackers to perform AD enumeration.

rules.title: Add Windows Capability Via PowerShell Cmdlet
rules.description: Detects usage of the "Add-WindowsCapability" cmdlet to add Windows capabilities. Notable capabilities could be "OpenSSH" and others.

rules.title: Potential AMSI Bypass Via .NET Reflection
rules.description: Detects Request to "amsiInitFailed" that can be used to disable AMSI Scanning

rules.title: Potential AMSI Bypass Using NULL Bits
rules.description: Detects usage of special strings/null bits in order to potentially bypass AMSI functionalities

rules.title: Audio Capture via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects audio capture via PowerShell Cmdlet.

rules.title: Suspicious Encoded PowerShell Command Line
rules.description: Detects suspicious powershell process starts with base64 encoded commands (e.g. Emotet)

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Patterns
rules.description: Detects PowerShell command line patterns in combincation with encoded commands that often appear in malware infection chains

rules.title: Suspicious Obfuscated PowerShell Code
rules.description: Detects suspicious UTF16 and base64 encoded and often obfuscated PowerShell code often used in command lines

rules.title: Malicious Base64 Encoded PowerShell Keywords in Command Lines
rules.description: Detects base64 encoded strings used in hidden malicious PowerShell command lines

rules.title: PowerShell Base64 Encoded Invoke Keyword
rules.description: Detects UTF-8 and UTF-16 Base64 encoded powershell 'Invoke-' calls

rules.title: PowerShell Base64 Encoded Reflective Assembly Load
rules.description: Detects base64 encoded .NET reflective loading of Assembly

rules.title: Suspicious Encoded And Obfuscated Reflection Assembly Load Function Call
rules.description: Detects suspicious base64 encoded and obfuscated "LOAD" keyword used in .NET "reflection.assembly"

rules.title: PowerShell Base64 Encoded WMI Classes
rules.description: Detects calls to base64 encoded WMI class such as "Win32_ShadowCopy", "Win32_ScheduledJob", etc.

rules.title: Potential Process Execution Proxy Via CL_Invocation.ps1
rules.description: Detects calls to "SyncInvoke" that is part of the "CL_Invocation.ps1" script to proxy execution using "System.Diagnostics.Process"

rules.title: Assembly Loading Via CL_LoadAssembly.ps1
rules.description: Detects calls to "LoadAssemblyFromPath" or "LoadAssemblyFromNS" that are part of the "CL_LoadAssembly.ps1" script. This can be abused to load different assemblies and bypass App locker controls.

rules.title: Potential Script Proxy Execution Via CL_Mutexverifiers.ps1
rules.description: Detects the use of the Microsoft signed script "CL_mutexverifiers" to proxy the execution of additional PowerShell script commands

rules.title: ConvertTo-SecureString Cmdlet Usage Via CommandLine
rules.description: Detects usage of the "ConvertTo-SecureString" cmdlet via the commandline. Which is fairly uncommon and could indicate potential suspicious activity

rules.title: Potential PowerShell Obfuscation Via Reversed Commands
rules.description: Detects the presence of reversed PowerShell commands in the CommandLine. This is often used as a method of obfuscation by attackers

rules.title: Potential PowerShell Command Line Obfuscation
rules.description: Detects the PowerShell command lines with special characters

values: public static extern bool InstallELAMCertificateInfo(SafeFileHandle handle);

rules.title: Computer Discovery And Export Via Get-ADComputer Cmdlet
rules.description: Detects usage of the Get-ADComputer cmdlet to collect computer information and output it to a file

rules.title: New Service Creation Using PowerShell
rules.description: Detects the creation of a new service using powershell.

rules.title: Gzip Archive Decode Via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects attempts of decoding encoded Gzip archives via PowerShell.

rules.title: PowerShell Execution With Potential Decryption Capabilities
rules.description: Detects PowerShell commands that decrypt an ".LNK" "file to drop the next stage of the malware.

rules.title: Powershell Defender Exclusion
rules.description: Detects requests to exclude files, folders or processes from Antivirus scanning using PowerShell cmdlets

rules.title: Disable Windows Defender AV Security Monitoring
rules.description: Detects attackers attempting to disable Windows Defender using Powershell

rules.title: Windows Firewall Disabled via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects attempts to disable the Windows Firewall using PowerShell

rules.title: Disabled IE Security Features
rules.description: Detects command lines that indicate unwanted modifications to registry keys that disable important Internet Explorer security features

rules.title: Potential PowerShell Downgrade Attack
rules.description: Detects PowerShell downgrade attack by comparing the host versions with the actually used engine version 2.0

rules.title: Potential COM Objects Download Cradles Usage - Process Creation
rules.description: Detects usage of COM objects that can be abused to download files in PowerShell by CLSID

rules.title: Obfuscated PowerShell OneLiner Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a specific OneLiner to download and execute powershell modules in memory.

rules.title: Potential DLL File Download Via PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest
rules.description: Detects potential DLL files being downloaded using the PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest or Invoke-RestMethod cmdlets.

rules.title: PowerShell Download and Execution Cradles
rules.description: Detects PowerShell download and execution cradles.

rules.title: PowerShell Download Pattern
rules.description: Detects a Powershell process that contains download commands in its command line string

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious File Download From File Sharing Domain Via PowerShell.EXE
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious file downloads from file sharing domains using PowerShell.exe

rules.title: DSInternals Suspicious PowerShell Cmdlets
rules.description:
Detects execution and usage of the DSInternals PowerShell module. Which can be used to perform what might be considered as suspicious activity such as dumping DPAPI backup keys or manipulating NTDS.DIT files.
The DSInternals PowerShell Module exposes several internal features of Active Directory and Azure Active Directory. These include FIDO2 and NGC key auditing, offline ntds.dit file manipulation, password auditing, DC recovery from IFM backups and password hash calculation.

rules.title: Email Exifiltration Via Powershell
rules.description: Detects email exfiltration via powershell cmdlets

rules.title: Potential Suspicious Windows Feature Enabled - ProcCreation
rules.description:
Detects usage of the built-in PowerShell cmdlet "Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature" used as a Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool.
Similar to DISM.exe, this cmdlet is used to enumerate, install, uninstall, configure, and update features and packages in Windows images

rules.title: Suspicious Execution of Powershell with Base64
rules.description: Commandline to launch powershell with a base64 payload

rules.title: Potential Encoded PowerShell Patterns In CommandLine
rules.description: Detects specific combinations of encoding methods in PowerShell via the commandline

rules.title: Powershell Inline Execution From A File
rules.description: Detects inline execution of PowerShell code from a file

rules.title: Certificate Exported Via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects calls to cmdlets that are used to export certificates from the local certificate store. Threat actors were seen abusing this to steal private keys from compromised machines.

rules.title: Base64 Encoded PowerShell Command Detected
rules.description: Detects usage of the "FromBase64String" function in the commandline which is used to decode a base64 encoded string

rules.title: Suspicious FromBase64String Usage On Gzip Archive - Process Creation
rules.description: Detects attempts of decoding a base64 Gzip archive via PowerShell. This technique is often used as a method to load malicious content into memory afterward.

rules.title: PowerShell Get-Clipboard Cmdlet Via CLI
rules.description: Detects usage of the 'Get-Clipboard' cmdlet via CLI

rules.title: Suspicious Reconnaissance Activity Using Get-LocalGroupMember Cmdlet
rules.description: Detects suspicious reconnaissance command line activity on Windows systems using the PowerShell Get-LocalGroupMember Cmdlet

rules.title: PowerShell Get-Process LSASS
rules.description: Detects a "Get-Process" cmdlet and it's aliases on lsass process, which is in almost all cases a sign of malicious activity

rules.title: Abuse of Service Permissions to Hide Services Via Set-Service
rules.description: Detects usage of the "Set-Service" powershell cmdlet to configure a new SecurityDescriptor that allows a service to be hidden from other utilities such as "sc.exe", "Get-Service"...etc. (Works only in powershell 7)

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell IEX Execution Patterns
rules.description: Detects suspicious ways to run Invoke-Execution using IEX alias

rules.title: Root Certificate Installed From Susp Locations
rules.description: Adversaries may install a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.

rules.title: Import PowerShell Modules From Suspicious Directories - ProcCreation
rules.description: Detects powershell scripts that import modules from suspicious directories

rules.title: Unsigned AppX Installation Attempt Using Add-AppxPackage
rules.description: Detects usage of the "Add-AppxPackage" or it's alias "Add-AppPackage" to install unsigned AppX packages

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Invocations - Specific - ProcessCreation
rules.description: Detects suspicious PowerShell invocation command parameters

rules.title: Suspicious Invoke-WebRequest Execution With DirectIP
rules.description: Detects calls to PowerShell with Invoke-WebRequest cmdlet using direct IP access

rules.title: Suspicious Invoke-WebRequest Execution
rules.description: Detects a suspicious call to Invoke-WebRequest cmdlet where the and output is located in a suspicious location

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Mailbox Export to Share
rules.description: Detects usage of the powerShell New-MailboxExportRequest Cmdlet to exports a mailbox to a remote or local share, as used in ProxyShell exploitations

rules.title: Malicious PowerShell Commandlets - ProcessCreation
rules.description: Detects Commandlet names from well-known PowerShell exploitation frameworks

rules.title: MSExchange Transport Agent Installation
rules.description: Detects the Installation of a Exchange Transport Agent

rules.title: Non Interactive PowerShell Process Spawned
rules.description: Detects non-interactive PowerShell activity by looking at the "powershell" process with a non-user GUI process such as "explorer.exe" as a parent.

rules.title: Potential PowerShell Obfuscation Via WCHAR/CHAR
rules.description: Detects suspicious encoded character syntax often used for defense evasion

rules.title: Execution of Powershell Script in Public Folder
rules.description: This rule detects execution of PowerShell scripts located in the "C:\Users\Public" folder

rules.title: RemoteFXvGPUDisablement Abuse Via AtomicTestHarnesses
rules.description: Detects calls to the AtomicTestHarnesses "Invoke-ATHRemoteFXvGPUDisablementCommand" which is designed to abuse the "RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.exe" binary to run custom PowerShell code via module load-order hijacking.

rules.title: Tamper Windows Defender Remove-MpPreference
rules.description: Detects attempts to remove Windows Defender configurations using the 'MpPreference' cmdlet

rules.title: Potential Powershell ReverseShell Connection
rules.description: Detects usage of the "TcpClient" class. Which can be abused to establish remote connections and reverse-shells. As seen used by the Nishang "Invoke-PowerShellTcpOneLine" reverse shell and other.

rules.title: Run PowerShell Script from ADS
rules.description: Detects PowerShell script execution from Alternate Data Stream (ADS)

rules.title: Run PowerShell Script from Redirected Input Stream
rules.description: Detects PowerShell script execution via input stream redirect

rules.title: PowerShell SAM Copy
rules.description: Detects suspicious PowerShell scripts accessing SAM hives

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Invocation From Script Engines
rules.description: Detects suspicious powershell invocations from interpreters or unusual programs

rules.title: Suspicious Service DACL Modification Via Set-Service Cmdlet
rules.description: Detects suspicious DACL modifications via the "Set-Service" cmdlet using the "SecurityDescriptorSddl" flag (Only available with PowerShell 7) that can be used to hide services or make them unstopable

rules.title: PowerShell Script Change Permission Via Set-Acl
rules.description: Detects PowerShell execution to set the ACL of a file or a folder

rules.title: PowerShell Set-Acl On Windows Folder
rules.description: Detects PowerShell scripts to set the ACL to a file in the Windows folder

rules.title: Change PowerShell Policies to an Insecure Level
rules.description: Detects changing the PowerShell script execution policy to a potentially insecure level using the "-ExecutionPolicy" flag.

rules.title: Service StartupType Change Via PowerShell Set-Service
rules.description: Detects the use of the PowerShell "Set-Service" cmdlet to change the startup type of a service to "disabled" or "manual"

rules.title: Deletion of Volume Shadow Copies via WMI with PowerShell
rules.description: Detects deletion of Windows Volume Shadow Copies with PowerShell code and Get-WMIObject. This technique is used by numerous ransomware families such as Sodinokibi/REvil

rules.title: Exchange PowerShell Snap-Ins Usage
rules.description: Detects adding and using Exchange PowerShell snap-ins to export mailbox data. As seen used by HAFNIUM and APT27

rules.title: Stop Windows Service Via PowerShell Stop-Service
rules.description: Detects the stopping of a Windows service via the PowerShell Cmdlet "Stop-Service"

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Download and Execute Pattern
rules.description: Detects suspicious PowerShell download patterns that are often used in malicious scripts, stagers or downloaders (make sure that your backend applies the strings case-insensitive)

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Parameter Substring
rules.description: Detects suspicious PowerShell invocation with a parameter substring

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Parent Process
rules.description: Detects a suspicious or uncommon parent processes of PowerShell

rules.title: PowerShell Script Run in AppData
rules.description: Detects a suspicious command line execution that invokes PowerShell with reference to an AppData folder

rules.title: Powershell Token Obfuscation - Process Creation
rules.description: Detects TOKEN OBFUSCATION technique from Invoke-Obfuscation

rules.title: User Discovery And Export Via Get-ADUser Cmdlet
rules.description: Detects usage of the Get-ADUser cmdlet to collect user information and output it to a file

rules.title: Net WebClient Casing Anomalies
rules.description: Detects PowerShell command line contents that include a suspicious abnormal casing in the Net.Webclient (e.g. nEt.WEbCliEnT) string as used in obfuscation techniques

rules.title: Suspicious X509Enrollment - Process Creation
rules.description: Detect use of X509Enrollment

rules.title: Suspicious XOR Encoded PowerShell Command
rules.description: Detects presence of a potentially xor encoded powershell command

rules.title: Folder Compress To Potentially Suspicious Output Via Compress-Archive Cmdlet
rules.description:
Detects PowerShell scripts that make use of the "Compress-Archive" Cmdlet in order to compress folders and files where the output is stored in a potentially suspicious location that is used often by malware for exfiltration.
An adversary might compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.

rules.title: Arbitrary File Download Via PresentationHost.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of "PresentationHost" which is a utility that runs ".xbap" (Browser Applications) files to download arbitrary files

rules.title: XBAP Execution From Uncommon Locations Via PresentationHost.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of ".xbap" (Browser Applications) files via PresentationHost.EXE from an uncommon location. These files can be abused to run malicious ".xbap" files any bypass AWL

rules.title: Visual Studio NodejsTools PressAnyKey Arbitrary Binary Execution
rules.description: Detects child processes of Microsoft.NodejsTools.PressAnyKey.exe that can be used to execute any other binary

rules.title: Abusing Print Executable
rules.description: Attackers can use print.exe for remote file copy

rules.title: File Download Using ProtocolHandler.exe
rules.description: Detects usage of "ProtocolHandler" to download files. Downloaded files will be located in the cache folder (for example - %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\INetCache\IE)

rules.title: Potential Provlaunch.EXE Binary Proxy Execution Abuse
rules.description: Detects child processes of "provlaunch.exe" which might indicate potential abuse to proxy execution.

rules.title: Suspicious Provlaunch.EXE Child Process
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes of "provlaunch.exe" which might indicate potential abuse to proxy execution.

rules.title: Screen Capture Activity Via Psr.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of Windows Problem Steps Recorder (psr.exe), a utility used to record the user screen and clicks.

rules.title: PUA - 3Proxy Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of 3proxy, a tiny free proxy server

rules.title: PUA - Suspicious ActiveDirectory Enumeration Via AdFind.EXE
rules.description: Detects active directory enumeration activity using known AdFind CLI flags

rules.title: PUA - AdFind Suspicious Execution
rules.description: Detects AdFind execution with common flags seen used during attacks

rules.title: PUA - Advanced IP Scanner Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of Advanced IP Scanner. Seems to be a popular tool for ransomware groups.

rules.title: PUA - Advanced Port Scanner Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of Advanced Port Scanner.

rules.title: PUA - AdvancedRun Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of AdvancedRun utility

rules.title: PUA - AdvancedRun Suspicious Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of AdvancedRun utility in the context of the TrustedInstaller, SYSTEM, Local Service or Network Service accounts

rules.title: PUA - Chisel Tunneling Tool Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of the Chisel tunneling tool via the commandline arguments

rules.title: PUA - CleanWipe Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of CleanWipe a tool usually used to delete Symantec antivirus.

rules.title: PUA - Crassus Execution
rules.description: Detects Crassus, a Windows privilege escalation discovery tool, based on PE metadata characteristics.

rules.title: PUA - CsExec Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of the lesser known remote execution tool named CsExec a PsExec alternative

rules.title: PUA - DefenderCheck Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of DefenderCheck, a tool to evaluate the signatures used in Microsoft Defender. It can be used to figure out the strings / byte chains used in Microsoft Defender to detect a tool and thus used for AV evasion.

rules.title: PUA - DIT Snapshot Viewer
rules.description: Detects the use of Ditsnap tool, an inspection tool for Active Directory database, ntds.dit.

rules.title: PUA - Fast Reverse Proxy (FRP) Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of Fast Reverse Proxy. frp is a fast reverse proxy to help you expose a local server behind a NAT or firewall to the Internet.

rules.title: PUA- IOX Tunneling Tool Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of IOX - a tool for port forwarding and intranet proxy purposes

rules.title: PUA - Mouse Lock Execution
rules.description: In Kaspersky's 2020 Incident Response Analyst Report they listed legitimate tool "Mouse Lock" as being used for both credential access and collection in security incidents.

rules.title: PUA - Netcat Suspicious Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of Netcat. Adversaries may use a non-application layer protocol for communication between host and C2 server or among infected hosts within a network

rules.title: PUA - SoftPerfect Netscan Execution
rules.description:
Detects usage of SoftPerfect's "netscan.exe". An application for scanning networks.
It is actively used in-the-wild by threat actors to inspect and understand the network architecture of a victim.

rules.title: PUA - Ngrok Execution
rules.description:
Detects the use of Ngrok, a utility used for port forwarding and tunneling, often used by threat actors to make local protected services publicly available.
Involved domains are bin.equinox.io for download and *.ngrok.io for connections.

rules.title: PUA - Nimgrab Execution
rules.description: Detects the usage of nimgrab, a tool bundled with the Nim programming framework and used for downloading files.

rules.title: PUA - NimScan Execution
rules.description:
Detects usage of NimScan, a portscanner utility.
In early 2025, adversaries were observed using this utility to scan for open ports on remote hosts in a compromised environment.
This rule identifies the execution of NimScan based on the process image name and specific hash values associated with different versions of the tool.

rules.title: PUA - NirCmd Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of NirCmd tool for command execution, which could be the result of legitimate administrative activity

rules.title: PUA - NirCmd Execution As LOCAL SYSTEM
rules.description: Detects the use of NirCmd tool for command execution as SYSTEM user

rules.title: PUA - Nmap/Zenmap Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of namp/zenmap. Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts, including those that may be vulnerable to remote software exploitation

rules.title: PUA - NPS Tunneling Tool Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of NPS, a port forwarding and intranet penetration proxy server

rules.title: PUA - NSudo Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of NSudo tool for command execution

rules.title: PUA - PingCastle Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of PingCastle, a tool designed to quickly assess the Active Directory security level.

rules.title: PUA - PingCastle Execution From Potentially Suspicious Parent
rules.description: Detects the execution of PingCastle, a tool designed to quickly assess the Active Directory security level via a script located in a potentially suspicious or uncommon location.

rules.title: PUA - Process Hacker Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of Process Hacker based on binary metadata information (Image, Hash, Imphash, etc).
Process Hacker is a tool to view and manipulate processes, kernel options and other low level options.
Threat actors abused older vulnerable versions to manipulate system processes.

rules.title: PUA - Radmin Viewer Utility Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of Radmin which can be abused by an adversary to remotely control Windows machines

rules.title: PUA - Potential PE Metadata Tamper Using Rcedit
rules.description: Detects the use of rcedit to potentially alter executable PE metadata properties, which could conceal efforts to rename system utilities for defense evasion.

rules.title: PUA - Rclone Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of RClone utility for exfiltration as used by various ransomwares strains like REvil, Conti, FiveHands, etc

rules.title: PUA - RunXCmd Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of the RunXCmd tool to execute commands with System or TrustedInstaller accounts

rules.title: PUA - Seatbelt Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of the PUA/Recon tool Seatbelt via PE information of command line parameters

rules.title: PUA - System Informer Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of System Informer, a task manager tool to view and manipulate processes, kernel options and other low level operations

rules.title: PUA - WebBrowserPassView Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of WebBrowserPassView.exe. A password recovery tool that reveals the passwords stored by the following Web browsers, Internet Explorer (Version 4.0 - 11.0), Mozilla Firefox (All Versions), Google Chrome, Safari, and Opera

rules.title: PUA - Wsudo Suspicious Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of wsudo (Windows Sudo Utility). Which is a tool that let the user execute programs with different permissions (System, Trusted Installer, Administrator...etc)

rules.title: PUA - Adidnsdump Execution
rules.description:
This tool enables enumeration and exporting of all DNS records in the zone for recon purposes of internal networks Python 3 and python.exe must be installed,
Usee to Query/modify DNS records for Active Directory integrated DNS via LDAP

rules.title: Python Inline Command Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of python using the "-c" flag. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live python code.

rules.title: Python Spawning Pretty TTY on Windows
rules.description: Detects python spawning a pretty tty

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Usage Of Qemu
rules.description:
Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Qemu utility in a Windows environment.
Threat actors have leveraged this utility and this technique for achieving network access as reported by Kaspersky.

rules.title: Query Usage To Exfil Data
rules.description: Detects usage of "query.exe" a system binary to exfil information such as "sessions" and "processes" for later use

rules.title: Files Added To An Archive Using Rar.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of "rar" to add files to an archive for potential compression. An adversary may compress data (e.g. sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.

rules.title: Rar Usage with Password and Compression Level
rules.description: Detects the use of rar.exe, on the command line, to create an archive with password protection or with a specific compression level. This is pretty indicative of malicious actions.

rules.title: Suspicious Greedy Compression Using Rar.EXE
rules.description: Detects RAR usage that creates an archive from a suspicious folder, either a system folder or one of the folders often used by attackers for staging purposes

rules.title: Suspicious RASdial Activity
rules.description: Detects suspicious process related to rasdial.exe

rules.title: Process Memory Dump via RdrLeakDiag.EXE
rules.description: Detects the use of the Microsoft Windows Resource Leak Diagnostic tool "rdrleakdiag.exe" to dump process memory

rules.title: Potential Persistence Attempt Via Run Keys Using Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects suspicious command line reg.exe tool adding key to RUN key in Registry

rules.title: Add SafeBoot Keys Via Reg Utility
rules.description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" commands with the "add" or "copy" flags on safe boot registry keys. Often used by attacker to allow the ransomware to work in safe mode as some security products do not

rules.title: Suspicious Reg Add BitLocker
rules.description: Detects suspicious addition to BitLocker related registry keys via the reg.exe utility

rules.title: Dropping Of Password Filter DLL
rules.description: Detects dropping of dll files in system32 that may be used to retrieve user credentials from LSASS

rules.title: Suspicious Windows Defender Folder Exclusion Added Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" to add Defender folder exclusions. Qbot has been seen using this technique to add exclusions for folders within AppData and ProgramData.

rules.title: SafeBoot Registry Key Deleted Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" commands with the "delete" flag on safe boot registry keys. Often used by attacker to prevent safeboot execution of security products

rules.title: Service Registry Key Deleted Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" commands with the "delete" flag on services registry key. Often used by attacker to remove AV software services

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Using Reg.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of "reg.exe" to alter registry keys that would replace the user's desktop background.
This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.

rules.title: Direct Autorun Keys Modification
rules.description: Detects direct modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry using reg.exe.

rules.title: Security Service Disabled Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" to disable security services such as Windows Defender.

rules.title: Dumping of Sensitive Hives Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to dump sensitive registry hives. This includes SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY hives.

rules.title: Windows Recall Feature Enabled Via Reg.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the enabling of the Windows Recall feature via registry manipulation.
Windows Recall can be enabled by deleting the existing "DisableAIDataAnalysis" value, or setting it to 0.
Adversaries may enable Windows Recall as part of post-exploitation discovery and collection activities.
This rule assumes that Recall is already explicitly disabled on the host, and subsequently enabled by the adversary.

rules.title: Enumeration for Credentials in Registry
rules.description:
Adversaries may search the Registry on compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials.
The Windows Registry stores configuration information that can be used by the system or other programs.
Adversaries may query the Registry looking for credentials and passwords that have been stored for use by other programs or services

rules.title: Potential Suspicious Registry File Imported Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the import of '.reg' files from suspicious paths using the 'reg.exe' utility

rules.title: RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation
rules.description:
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise

rules.title: LSA PPL Protection Disabled Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of the "reg.exe" utility to disable PPL protection on the LSA process

rules.title: Suspicious Query of MachineGUID
rules.description: Use of reg to get MachineGuid information

rules.title: Modify Group Policy Settings
rules.description: Detect malicious GPO modifications can be used to implement many other malicious behaviors.

rules.title: Enable LM Hash Storage - ProcCreation
rules.description:
Detects changes to the "NoLMHash" registry value in order to allow Windows to store LM Hashes.
By setting this registry value to "0" (DWORD), Windows will be allowed to store a LAN manager hash of your password in Active Directory and local SAM databases.

rules.title: Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to query reconnaissance information from the registry. Adversaries may interact with the Windows registry to gather information about credentials, the system, configuration, and installed software.

rules.title: Potential Tampering With RDP Related Registry Keys Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of "reg.exe" for enabling/disabling the RDP service on the host by tampering with the 'CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server' values

rules.title: Suspicious ScreenSave Change by Reg.exe
rules.description:
Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by user inactivity.
Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension

rules.title: Changing Existing Service ImagePath Value Via Reg.EXE
rules.description:
Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services.
Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start.
Windows stores local service configuration information in the Registry under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services

rules.title: Detected Windows Software Discovery
rules.description: Adversaries may attempt to enumerate software for a variety of reasons, such as figuring out what security measures are present or if the compromised system has a version of software that is vulnerable.

rules.title: Reg Add Suspicious Paths
rules.description: Detects when an adversary uses the reg.exe utility to add or modify new keys or subkeys

rules.title: Disabled Volume Snapshots
rules.description: Detects commands that temporarily turn off Volume Snapshots

rules.title: Suspicious Windows Defender Registry Key Tampering Via Reg.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" to tamper with different Windows Defender registry keys in order to disable some important features related to protection and detection

rules.title: Write Protect For Storage Disabled
rules.description:
Detects applications trying to modify the registry in order to disable any write-protect property for storage devices.
This could be a precursor to a ransomware attack and has been an observed technique used by cypherpunk group.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs With Uncommon Extension
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Regasm/Regsvcs utilities with an uncommon extension.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs From Uncommon Location
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Regasm/Regsvcs utilities from a potentially suspicious location

rules.title: Exports Critical Registry Keys To a File
rules.description: Detects the export of a crital Registry key to a file.

rules.title: Exports Registry Key To a File
rules.description: Detects the export of the target Registry key to a file.

rules.title: Imports Registry Key From a File
rules.description: Detects the import of the specified file to the registry with regedit.exe.

rules.title: Imports Registry Key From an ADS
rules.description: Detects the import of a alternate datastream to the registry with regedit.exe.

rules.title: Regedit as Trusted Installer
rules.description: Detects a regedit started with TrustedInstaller privileges or by ProcessHacker.exe

rules.title: Suspicious Registry Modification From ADS Via Regini.EXE
rules.description: Detects the import of an alternate data stream with regini.exe, regini.exe can be used to modify registry keys.

rules.title: Registry Modification Via Regini.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of regini.exe which can be used to modify registry keys, the changes are imported from one or more text files.

rules.title: DLL Execution Via Register-cimprovider.exe
rules.description: Detects using register-cimprovider.exe to execute arbitrary dll file.

rules.title: Enumeration for 3rd Party Creds From CLI
rules.description: Detects processes that query known 3rd party registry keys that holds credentials via commandline

rules.title: IE ZoneMap Setting Downgraded To MyComputer Zone For HTTP Protocols Via CLI
rules.description: Detects changes to Internet Explorer's (IE / Windows Internet properties) ZoneMap configuration of the "HTTP" and "HTTPS" protocols to point to the "My Computer" zone. This allows downloaded files from the Internet to be granted the same level of trust as files stored locally.

rules.title: Suspicious Debugger Registration Cmdline
rules.description: Detects the registration of a debugger for a program that is available in the logon screen (sticky key backdoor).

rules.title: Potential Persistence Via Logon Scripts - CommandLine
rules.description: Detects the addition of a new LogonScript to the registry value "UserInitMprLogonScript" for potential persistence

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - CLI
rules.description: Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it

rules.title: Python Function Execution Security Warning Disabled In Excel
rules.description:
Detects changes to the registry value "PythonFunctionWarnings" that would prevent any warnings or alerts from showing when Python functions are about to be executed.
Threat actors could run malicious code through the new Microsoft Excel feature that allows Python to run within the spreadsheet.

rules.title: Potential Privilege Escalation via Service Permissions Weakness
rules.description: Detect modification of services configuration (ImagePath, FailureCommand and ServiceDLL) in registry by processes with Medium integrity level

rules.title: Potential Provisioning Registry Key Abuse For Binary Proxy Execution
rules.description: Detects potential abuse of the provisioning registry key for indirect command execution through "Provlaunch.exe".

rules.title: Potential PowerShell Execution Policy Tampering - ProcCreation
rules.description: Detects changes to the PowerShell execution policy registry key in order to bypass signing requirements for script execution from the CommandLine

rules.title: Hiding User Account Via SpecialAccounts Registry Key - CommandLine
rules.description: Detects changes to the registry key "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\SpecialAccounts\Userlist" where the value is set to "0" in order to hide user account from being listed on the logon screen.

rules.title: Persistence Via TypedPaths - CommandLine
rules.description: Detects modification addition to the 'TypedPaths' key in the user or admin registry via the commandline. Which might indicate persistence attempt

rules.title: Potential Regsvr32 Commandline Flag Anomaly
rules.description: Detects a potential command line flag anomaly related to "regsvr32" in which the "/i" flag is used without the "/n" which should be uncommon.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP IP Pattern
rules.description: Detects regsvr32 execution to download and install DLLs located remotely where the address is an IP address.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP/FTP Pattern
rules.description: Detects regsvr32 execution to download/install/register new DLLs that are hosted on Web or FTP servers.

rules.title: Suspicious Regsvr32 Execution From Remote Share
rules.description: Detects REGSVR32.exe to execute DLL hosted on remote shares

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of Regsvr32
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "regsvr32.exe".

rules.title: Regsvr32 Execution From Potential Suspicious Location
rules.description: Detects execution of regsvr32 where the DLL is located in a potentially suspicious location.

rules.title: Regsvr32 Execution From Highly Suspicious Location
rules.description: Detects execution of regsvr32 where the DLL is located in a highly suspicious locations

rules.title: Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Suspicious File Extension
rules.description: Detects the execution of REGSVR32.exe with DLL files masquerading as other files

rules.title: Scripting/CommandLine Process Spawned Regsvr32
rules.description: Detects various command line and scripting engines/processes such as "PowerShell", "Wscript", "Cmd", etc. spawning a "regsvr32" instance.

rules.title: Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension
rules.description: Detects a "regsvr32" execution where the DLL doesn't contain a common file extension.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Execution
rules.description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Piped Password Via CLI
rules.description: Detects piping the password to an anydesk instance via CMD and the '--set-password' flag.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Silent Installation
rules.description: Detects AnyDesk Remote Desktop silent installation. Which can be used by attackers to gain remote access.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - Anydesk Execution From Suspicious Folder
rules.description:

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - MeshAgent Command Execution via MeshCentral
rules.description:
Detects the use of MeshAgent to execute commands on the target host, particularly when threat actors might abuse it to execute commands directly.
MeshAgent can execute commands on the target host by leveraging win-console to obscure their activities and win-dispatcher to run malicious code through IPC with child processes.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - NetSupport Execution From Unusual Location
rules.description: Detects execution of client32.exe (NetSupport RAT) from an unusual location (outside of 'C:\Program Files')

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - RURAT Execution From Unusual Location
rules.description: Detects execution of Remote Utilities RAT (RURAT) from an unusual location (outside of 'C:\Program Files')

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Installation Execution
rules.description: Detects ScreenConnect program starts that establish a remote access to a system.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Remote Command Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a system command via the ScreenConnect RMM service.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Potential Suspicious Remote Command Execution
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes launched via the ScreenConnect client service.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Server Web Shell Execution
rules.description: Detects potential web shell execution from the ScreenConnect server process.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - Team Viewer Session Started On Windows Host
rules.description:
Detects the command line executed when TeamViewer starts a session started by a remote host.
Once a connection has been started, an investigator can verify the connection details by viewing the "incoming_connections.txt" log file in the TeamViewer folder.

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - UltraViewer Execution

rules.title: Discovery of a System Time
rules.description: Identifies use of various commands to query a systems time. This technique may be used before executing a scheduled task or to discover the time zone of a target system.

rules.title: Renamed AdFind Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of a renamed Adfind.exe. AdFind continues to be seen across majority of breaches. It is used to domain trust discovery to plan out subsequent steps in the attack chain.

rules.title: Renamed AutoHotkey.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of a renamed autohotkey.exe binary based on PE metadata fields

rules.title: Renamed AutoIt Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of a renamed AutoIt2.exe or AutoIt3.exe.
AutoIt is a scripting language and automation tool for Windows systems. While primarily used for legitimate automation tasks, it can be misused in cyber attacks.
Attackers can leverage AutoIt to create and distribute malware, including keyloggers, spyware, and botnets. A renamed AutoIt executable is particularly suspicious.

rules.title: Potential Defense Evasion Via Binary Rename
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed binary often used by attackers or malware leveraging new Sysmon OriginalFileName datapoint.

rules.title: Potential Defense Evasion Via Rename Of Highly Relevant Binaries
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed binary often used by attackers or malware leveraging new Sysmon OriginalFileName datapoint.

rules.title: Renamed BOINC Client Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed BOINC binary.

rules.title: Renamed BrowserCore.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects process creation with a renamed BrowserCore.exe (used to extract Azure tokens)

rules.title: Renamed Cloudflared.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "cloudflared" binary.

rules.title: Renamed CreateDump Utility Execution
rules.description: Detects uses of a renamed legitimate createdump.exe LOLOBIN utility to dump process memory

rules.title: Renamed CURL.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "CURL.exe" binary based on the PE metadata fields

rules.title: Renamed ZOHO Dctask64 Execution
rules.description:
Detects a renamed "dctask64.exe" execution, a signed binary by ZOHO Corporation part of ManageEngine Endpoint Central.
This binary can be abused for DLL injection, arbitrary command and process execution.

rules.title: Renamed FTP.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "ftp.exe" binary based on the PE metadata fields

rules.title: Renamed Gpg.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "gpg.exe". Often used by ransomware and loaders to decrypt/encrypt data.

rules.title: Renamed Jusched.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "jusched.exe" as seen used by the cobalt group

rules.title: Renamed Mavinject.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed version of the "Mavinject" process. Which can be abused to perform process injection using the "/INJECTRUNNING" flag

rules.title: Renamed MegaSync Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed MegaSync.exe as seen used by ransomware families like Nefilim, Sodinokibi, Pysa, and Conti.

rules.title: Renamed Msdt.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "Msdt.exe" binary

rules.title: Renamed Microsoft Teams Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed Microsoft Teams binary.

rules.title: Renamed NetSupport RAT Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "client32.exe" (NetSupport RAT) via Imphash, Product and OriginalFileName strings

rules.title: Renamed NirCmd.EXE Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "NirCmd.exe" binary based on the PE metadata fields.

rules.title: Renamed Office Binary Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed office binary

rules.title: Renamed PAExec Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of renamed version of PAExec. Often used by attackers

rules.title: Renamed PingCastle Binary Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed "PingCastle" binary based on the PE metadata fields.

rules.title: Renamed Plink Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a renamed version of the Plink binary

rules.title: Visual Studio NodejsTools PressAnyKey Renamed Execution
rules.description: Detects renamed execution of "Microsoft.NodejsTools.PressAnyKey.exe", which can be abused as a LOLBIN to execute arbitrary binaries

rules.title: Potential Renamed Rundll32 Execution
rules.description: Detects when 'DllRegisterServer' is called in the commandline and the image is not rundll32. This could mean that the 'rundll32' utility has been renamed in order to avoid detection

rules.title: Renamed Remote Utilities RAT (RURAT) Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of renamed Remote Utilities (RURAT) via Product PE header field

rules.title: Renamed SysInternals DebugView Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious renamed SysInternals DebugView execution

rules.title: Renamed ProcDump Execution
rules.description:
Detects the execution of a renamed ProcDump executable.
This often done by attackers or malware in order to evade defensive mechanisms.

rules.title: Renamed PsExec Service Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious launch of a renamed version of the PSEXESVC service with, which is not often used by legitimate administrators

rules.title: Renamed Sysinternals Sdelete Execution
rules.description: Detects the use of a renamed SysInternals Sdelete, which is something an administrator shouldn't do (the renaming)

rules.title: Renamed Vmnat.exe Execution
rules.description: Detects renamed vmnat.exe or portable version that can be used for DLL side-loading

rules.title: Renamed Whoami Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of whoami that has been renamed to a different name to avoid detection

rules.title: Capture Credentials with Rpcping.exe
rules.description: Detects using Rpcping.exe to send a RPC test connection to the target server (-s) and force the NTLM hash to be sent in the process.

rules.title: Ruby Inline Command Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of ruby using the "-e" flag. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live ruby code.

rules.title: Potential Rundll32 Execution With DLL Stored In ADS
rules.description: Detects execution of rundll32 where the DLL being called is stored in an Alternate Data Stream (ADS).

rules.title: Suspicious Advpack Call Via Rundll32.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "rundll32" calling "advpack.dll" with potential obfuscated ordinal calls in order to leverage the "RegisterOCX" function

rules.title: Suspicious Rundll32 Invoking Inline VBScript
rules.description: Detects suspicious process related to rundll32 based on command line that invokes inline VBScript as seen being used by UNC2452

rules.title: Rundll32 InstallScreenSaver Execution
rules.description: An attacker may execute an application as a SCR File using rundll32.exe desk.cpl,InstallScreenSaver

rules.title: Suspicious Key Manager Access
rules.description: Detects the invocation of the Stored User Names and Passwords dialogue (Key Manager)

rules.title: Mshtml.DLL RunHTMLApplication Suspicious Usage
rules.description: Detects execution of commands that leverage the "mshtml.dll" RunHTMLApplication export to run arbitrary code via different protocol handlers (vbscript, javascript, file, http...)

rules.title: Rundll32 Execution Without CommandLine Parameters
rules.description: Detects suspicious start of rundll32.exe without any parameters as found in CobaltStrike beacon activity

rules.title: Suspicious NTLM Authentication on the Printer Spooler Service
rules.description: Detects a privilege elevation attempt by coercing NTLM authentication on the Printer Spooler service

rules.title: Potential Obfuscated Ordinal Call Via Rundll32
rules.description: Detects execution of "rundll32" with potential obfuscated ordinal calls

rules.title: Rundll32 Spawned Via Explorer.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "rundll32.exe" with a parent process of Explorer.exe. This has been observed by variants of Raspberry Robin, as first reported by Red Canary.

rules.title: Process Memory Dump Via Comsvcs.DLL
rules.description: Detects a process memory dump via "comsvcs.dll" using rundll32, covering multiple different techniques (ordinal, minidump function, etc.)

rules.title: Rundll32 Registered COM Objects
rules.description: load malicious registered COM objects

rules.title: Suspicious Process Start Locations
rules.description: Detects suspicious process run from unusual locations

rules.title: Suspicious Rundll32 Setupapi.dll Activity
rules.description: setupapi.dll library provide InstallHinfSection function for processing INF files. INF file may contain instructions allowing to create values in the registry, modify files and install drivers. This technique could be used to obtain persistence via modifying one of Run or RunOnce registry keys, run process or use other DLLs chain calls (see references) InstallHinfSection function in setupapi.dll calls runonce.exe executable regardless of actual content of INF file.

rules.title: Shell32 DLL Execution in Suspicious Directory
rules.description: Detects shell32.dll executing a DLL in a suspicious directory

rules.title: Potential ShellDispatch.DLL Functionality Abuse
rules.description: Detects potential "ShellDispatch.dll" functionality abuse to execute arbitrary binaries via "ShellExecute"

rules.title: RunDLL32 Spawning Explorer
rules.description: Detects RunDLL32.exe spawning explorer.exe as child, which is very uncommon, often observes Gamarue spawning the explorer.exe process in an unusual way

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Rundll32 Activity
rules.description: Detects suspicious execution of rundll32, with specific calls to some DLLs with known LOLBIN functionalities

rules.title: Suspicious Control Panel DLL Load
rules.description: Detects suspicious Rundll32 execution from control.exe as used by Equation Group and Exploit Kits

rules.title: Suspicious Rundll32 Execution With Image Extension
rules.description: Detects the execution of Rundll32.exe with DLL files masquerading as image files

rules.title: Suspicious Usage Of ShellExec_RunDLL
rules.description: Detects suspicious usage of the ShellExec_RunDLL function to launch other commands as seen in the the raspberry-robin attack

rules.title: Suspicious ShellExec_RunDLL Call Via Ordinal
rules.description:
Detects suspicious call to the "ShellExec_RunDLL" exported function of SHELL32.DLL through the ordinal number to launch other commands.
Adversary might only use the ordinal number in order to bypass existing detection that alert on usage of ShellExec_RunDLL on CommandLine.

rules.title: ShimCache Flush
rules.description: Detects actions that clear the local ShimCache and remove forensic evidence

rules.title: Suspicious Rundll32 Activity Invoking Sys File
rules.description: Detects suspicious process related to rundll32 based on command line that includes a *.sys file as seen being used by UNC2452

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Rundll32.EXE Execution of UDL File
rules.description:
Detects the execution of rundll32.exe with the oledb32.dll library to open a UDL file.
Threat actors can abuse this technique as a phishing vector to capture authentication credentials or other sensitive data.

rules.title: Rundll32 UNC Path Execution
rules.description: Detects rundll32 execution where the DLL is located on a remote location (share)

rules.title: Rundll32 Execution With Uncommon DLL Extension
rules.description: Detects the execution of rundll32 with a command line that doesn't contain a common extension

rules.title: Suspicious Workstation Locking via Rundll32
rules.description: Detects a suspicious call to the user32.dll function that locks the user workstation

rules.title: WebDav Client Execution Via Rundll32.EXE
rules.description:
Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like "C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie".
This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on a WebDav server).

rules.title: Suspicious WebDav Client Execution Via Rundll32.EXE
rules.description: Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie. This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on WebDav Server) or potentially a sign of exploitation of CVE-2023-23397

rules.title: Rundll32 Execution Without Parameters
rules.description: Detects rundll32 execution without parameters as observed when running Metasploit windows/smb/psexec exploit module

rules.title: Run Once Task Execution as Configured in Registry
rules.description: This rule detects the execution of Run Once task as configured in the registry

rules.title: Possible Privilege Escalation via Weak Service Permissions
rules.description: Detection of sc.exe utility spawning by user with Medium integrity level to change service ImagePath or FailureCommand

rules.title: New Service Creation Using Sc.EXE
rules.description: Detects the creation of a new service using the "sc.exe" utility.

rules.title: Service StartupType Change Via Sc.EXE
rules.description: Detect the use of "sc.exe" to change the startup type of a service to "disabled" or "demand"

rules.title: New Kernel Driver Via SC.EXE
rules.description: Detects creation of a new service (kernel driver) with the type "kernel"

rules.title: Interesting Service Enumeration Via Sc.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the enumeration and query of interesting and in some cases sensitive services on the system via "sc.exe".
Attackers often try to enumerate the services currently running on a system in order to find different attack vectors.

rules.title: Allow Service Access Using Security Descriptor Tampering Via Sc.EXE
rules.description: Detects suspicious DACL modifications to allow access to a service from a suspicious trustee. This can be used to override access restrictions set by previous ACLs.

rules.title: Deny Service Access Using Security Descriptor Tampering Via Sc.EXE
rules.description: Detects suspicious DACL modifications to deny access to a service that affects critical trustees. This can be used to hide services or make them unstoppable.

rules.title: Service DACL Abuse To Hide Services Via Sc.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of the "sc.exe" utility adding a new service with special permission seen used by threat actors which makes the service hidden and unremovable.

rules.title: Service Security Descriptor Tampering Via Sc.EXE
rules.description: Detection of sc.exe utility adding a new service with special permission which hides that service.

rules.title: Suspicious Service Path Modification
rules.description: Detects service path modification via the "sc" binary to a suspicious command or path

rules.title: Potential Persistence Attempt Via Existing Service Tampering
rules.description: Detects the modification of an existing service in order to execute an arbitrary payload when the service is started or killed as a potential method for persistence.

rules.title: Stop Windows Service Via Sc.EXE
rules.description: Detects the stopping of a Windows service via the "sc.exe" utility

rules.title: Suspicious Schtasks Execution AppData Folder
rules.description: Detects the creation of a schtask that executes a file from C:\Users\<USER>\AppData\Local

rules.title: Suspicious Modification Of Scheduled Tasks
rules.description:
Detects when an attacker tries to modify an already existing scheduled tasks to run from a suspicious location
Attackers can create a simple looking task in order to avoid detection on creation as it's often the most focused on
Instead they modify the task after creation to include their malicious payload

rules.title: Scheduled Task Creation Via Schtasks.EXE
rules.description: Detects the creation of scheduled tasks by user accounts via the "schtasks" utility.

rules.title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation Involving Temp Folder
rules.description: Detects the creation of scheduled tasks that involves a temporary folder and runs only once

rules.title: Delete Important Scheduled Task
rules.description: Detects when adversaries stop services or processes by deleting their respective scheduled tasks in order to conduct data destructive activities

rules.title: Delete All Scheduled Tasks
rules.description: Detects the usage of schtasks with the delete flag and the asterisk symbol to delete all tasks from the schedule of the local computer, including tasks scheduled by other users.

rules.title: Disable Important Scheduled Task
rules.description: Detects when adversaries stop services or processes by disabling their respective scheduled tasks in order to conduct data destructive activities

rules.title: Schedule Task Creation From Env Variable Or Potentially Suspicious Path Via Schtasks.EXE
rules.description: Detects Schtask creations that point to a suspicious folder or an environment variable often used by malware

rules.title: Schtasks From Suspicious Folders
rules.description: Detects scheduled task creations that have suspicious action command and folder combinations

rules.title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Name As GUID
rules.description: Detects creation of a scheduled task with a GUID like name

rules.title: Uncommon One Time Only Scheduled Task At 00:00
rules.description: Detects scheduled task creation events that include suspicious actions, and is run once at 00:00

rules.title: Potential Persistence Via Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser
rules.description:
Detects manual execution of the "Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser" task via schtasks.
In order to trigger persistence stored in the "\AppCompatFlags\TelemetryController" registry key.

rules.title: Potential Persistence Via Powershell Search Order Hijacking - Task
rules.description: Detects suspicious powershell execution via a schedule task where the command ends with an suspicious flags to hide the powershell instance instead of executeing scripts or commands. This could be a sign of persistence via PowerShell "Get-Variable" technique as seen being used in Colibri Loader

rules.title: Scheduled Task Executing Payload from Registry
rules.description: Detects the creation of a schtasks that potentially executes a payload stored in the Windows Registry using PowerShell.

rules.title: Scheduled Task Executing Encoded Payload from Registry
rules.description: Detects the creation of a schtask that potentially executes a base64 encoded payload stored in the Windows Registry using PowerShell.

rules.title: Suspicious Schtasks Schedule Types
rules.description: Detects scheduled task creations or modification on a suspicious schedule type

rules.title: Suspicious Schtasks Schedule Type With High Privileges
rules.description: Detects scheduled task creations or modification to be run with high privileges on a suspicious schedule type

rules.title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation via Masqueraded XML File
rules.description: Detects the creation of a scheduled task using the "-XML" flag with a file without the '.xml' extension. This behavior could be indicative of potential defense evasion attempt during persistence

rules.title: Suspicious Command Patterns In Scheduled Task Creation
rules.description: Detects scheduled task creation using "schtasks" that contain potentially suspicious or uncommon commands

rules.title: Schtasks Creation Or Modification With SYSTEM Privileges
rules.description: Detects the creation or update of a scheduled task to run with "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" privileges

rules.title: Script Event Consumer Spawning Process
rules.description: Detects a suspicious child process of Script Event Consumer (scrcons.exe).

rules.title: Potential Shim Database Persistence via Sdbinst.EXE
rules.description:
Detects installation of a new shim using sdbinst.exe.
Adversaries may establish persistence and/or elevate privileges by executing malicious content triggered by application shims

rules.title: Uncommon Extension Shim Database Installation Via Sdbinst.EXE
rules.description:
Detects installation of a potentially suspicious new shim with an uncommon extension using sdbinst.exe.
Adversaries may establish persistence and/or elevate privileges by executing malicious content triggered by application shims

rules.title: Sdclt Child Processes
rules.description: A General detection for sdclt spawning new processes. This could be an indicator of sdclt being used for bypass UAC techniques.

rules.title: Sdiagnhost Calling Suspicious Child Process
rules.description: Detects sdiagnhost.exe calling a suspicious child process (e.g. used in exploits for Follina / CVE-2022-30190)

rules.title: Potential Suspicious Activity Using SeCEdit
rules.description: Detects potential suspicious behaviour using secedit.exe. Such as exporting or modifying the security policy

rules.title: Suspicious Serv-U Process Pattern
rules.description: Detects a suspicious process pattern which could be a sign of an exploited Serv-U service

rules.title: Uncommon Child Process Of Setres.EXE
rules.description:
Detects uncommon child process of Setres.EXE.
Setres.EXE is a Windows server only process and tool that can be used to set the screen resolution.
It can potentially be abused in order to launch any arbitrary file with a name containing the word "choice" from the current execution path.

rules.title: Potential SPN Enumeration Via Setspn.EXE
rules.description: Detects service principal name (SPN) enumeration used for Kerberoasting

rules.title: Setup16.EXE Execution With Custom .Lst File
rules.description:
Detects the execution of "Setup16.EXE" and old installation utility with a custom ".lst" file.
These ".lst" file can contain references to external program that "Setup16.EXE" will execute.
Attackers and adversaries might leverage this as a living of the land utility.

rules.title: Suspicious Execution of Shutdown
rules.description: Use of the commandline to shutdown or reboot windows

rules.title: Suspicious Execution of Shutdown to Log Out
rules.description: Detects the rare use of the command line tool shutdown to logoff a user

rules.title: Uncommon Sigverif.EXE Child Process
rules.description: Detects uncommon child processes spawning from "sigverif.exe", which could indicate potential abuse of the latter as a living of the land binary in order to proxy execution.

rules.title: Uncommon Child Processes Of SndVol.exe
rules.description: Detects potentially uncommon child processes of SndVol.exe (the Windows volume mixer)

rules.title: Audio Capture via SoundRecorder
rules.description: Detect attacker collecting audio via SoundRecorder application.

rules.title: Suspicious Splwow64 Without Params
rules.description: Detects suspicious Splwow64.exe process without any command line parameters

rules.title: Suspicious Spool Service Child Process
rules.description: Detects suspicious print spool service (spoolsv.exe) child processes.

rules.title: Veeam Backup Database Suspicious Query
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious SQL queries using SQLCmd targeting the Veeam backup databases in order to steal information.

rules.title: VeeamBackup Database Credentials Dump Via Sqlcmd.EXE
rules.description: Detects dump of credentials in VeeamBackup dbo

rules.title: SQLite Chromium Profile Data DB Access
rules.description: Detect usage of the "sqlite" binary to query databases in Chromium-based browsers for potential data stealing.

rules.title: SQLite Firefox Profile Data DB Access
rules.description: Detect usage of the "sqlite" binary to query databases in Firefox and other Gecko-based browsers for potential data stealing.

rules.title: Arbitrary File Download Via Squirrel.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of the "Squirrel.exe" to download arbitrary files. This binary is part of multiple Electron based software installations (Slack, Teams, Discord, etc.)

rules.title: Process Proxy Execution Via Squirrel.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of the "Squirrel.exe" binary to execute arbitrary processes. This binary is part of multiple Electron based software installations (Slack, Teams, Discord, etc.)

rules.title: Port Forwarding Activity Via SSH.EXE
rules.description: Detects port forwarding activity via SSH.exe

rules.title: Program Executed Using Proxy/Local Command Via SSH.EXE
rules.description: Detect usage of the "ssh.exe" binary as a proxy to launch other programs.

rules.title: Potential RDP Tunneling Via SSH
rules.description: Execution of ssh.exe to perform data exfiltration and tunneling through RDP

rules.title: Potential Amazon SSM Agent Hijacking
rules.description: Detects potential Amazon SSM agent hijack attempts as outlined in the Mitiga research report.

rules.title: Execution via stordiag.exe
rules.description: Detects the use of stordiag.exe to execute schtasks.exe systeminfo.exe and fltmc.exe

rules.title: Start of NT Virtual DOS Machine
