rules.description: Ntvdm.exe allows the execution of 16-bit Windows applications on 32-bit Windows operating systems, as well as the execution of both 16-bit and 32-bit DOS applications

rules.title: Abused Debug Privilege by Arbitrary Parent Processes
rules.description: Detection of unusual child processes by different system processes

rules.title: User Added to Local Administrators Group
rules.description: Detects addition of users to the local administrator group via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".

rules.title: User Added To Highly Privileged Group
rules.description: Detects addition of users to highly privileged groups via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".

rules.title: User Added to Remote Desktop Users Group
rules.description: Detects addition of users to the local Remote Desktop Users group via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".

rules.title: Execute From Alternate Data Streams
rules.description: Detects execution from an Alternate Data Stream (ADS). Adversaries may use NTFS file attributes to hide their malicious data in order to evade detection

rules.title: Always Install Elevated Windows Installer
rules.description: Detects Windows Installer service (msiexec.exe) trying to install MSI packages with SYSTEM privilege

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Windows App Activity
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child process of applications launched from inside the WindowsApps directory. This could be a sign of a rogue ".appx" package installation/execution

rules.title: Arbitrary Shell Command Execution Via Settingcontent-Ms
rules.description: The .SettingContent-ms file type was introduced in Windows 10 and allows a user to create "shortcuts" to various Windows 10 setting pages. These files are simply XML and contain paths to various Windows 10 settings binaries.

rules.title: Phishing Pattern ISO in Archive
rules.description: Detects cases in which an ISO files is opend within an archiver like 7Zip or Winrar, which is a sign of phishing as threat actors put small ISO files in archives as email attachments to bypass certain filters and protective measures (mark of web)

rules.title: Automated Collection Command Prompt
rules.description: Once established within a system or network, an adversary may use automated techniques for collecting internal data.

rules.title: Bad Opsec Defaults Sacrificial Processes With Improper Arguments
rules.description:
Detects attackers using tooling with bad opsec defaults.
E.g. spawning a sacrificial process to inject a capability into the process without taking into account how the process is normally run.
One trivial example of this is using rundll32.exe without arguments as a sacrificial process (default in CS, now highlighted by c2lint), running WerFault without arguments (Kraken - credit am0nsec), and other examples.

rules.title: Potential Suspicious Browser Launch From Document Reader Process
rules.description: Detects when a browser process or browser tab is launched from an application that handles document files such as Adobe, Microsoft Office, etc. And connects to a web application over http(s), this could indicate a possible phishing attempt.

rules.title: Potential Commandline Obfuscation Using Escape Characters
rules.description: Detects potential commandline obfuscation using known escape characters

rules.title: Potential CommandLine Obfuscation Using Unicode Characters From Suspicious Image
rules.description:
Detects potential commandline obfuscation using unicode characters.
Adversaries may attempt to make an executable or file difficult to discover or analyze by encrypting, encoding, or otherwise obfuscating its contents on the system or in transit.

rules.title: Potential Command Line Path Traversal Evasion Attempt
rules.description: Detects potential evasion or obfuscation attempts using bogus path traversal via the commandline

rules.title: Potential Browser Data Stealing
rules.description:
Adversaries may acquire credentials from web browsers by reading files specific to the target browser.
Web browsers commonly save credentials such as website usernames and passwords so that they do not need to be entered manually in the future.
Web browsers typically store the credentials in an encrypted format within a credential store.

rules.title: Copy From Or To Admin Share Or Sysvol Folder
rules.description: Detects a copy command or a copy utility execution to or from an Admin share or remote

rules.title: Suspicious Copy From or To System Directory
rules.description:
Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a program from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk.
Often used to move LOLBINs such as 'certutil' or 'desktopimgdownldr' to a different location with a different name in order to bypass detections based on locations.

rules.title: LOL-Binary Copied From System Directory
rules.description: Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a known LOLBIN from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk in order to bypass detections based on locations.

rules.title: Potential Crypto Mining Activity
rules.description: Detects command line parameters or strings often used by crypto miners

rules.title: Potential Data Exfiltration Activity Via CommandLine Tools
rules.description: Detects the use of various CLI utilities exfiltrating data via web requests

rules.title: Raccine Uninstall
rules.description: Detects commands that indicate a Raccine removal from an end system. Raccine is a free ransomware protection tool.

rules.title: Suspicious Double Extension File Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious use of an .exe extension after a non-executable file extension like .pdf.exe, a set of spaces or underlines to cloak the executable file in spear phishing campaigns

rules.title: Suspicious Parent Double Extension File Execution
rules.description: Detect execution of suspicious double extension files in ParentCommandLine

rules.title: Suspicious Download from Office Domain
rules.description: Detects suspicious ways to download files from Microsoft domains that are used to store attachments in Emails or OneNote documents

rules.title: DumpStack.log Defender Evasion
rules.description: Detects the use of the filename DumpStack.log to evade Microsoft Defender

rules.title: Always Install Elevated MSI Spawned Cmd And Powershell
rules.description: Detects Windows Installer service (msiexec.exe) spawning "cmd" or "powershell"

rules.title: Suspicious Electron Application Child Processes
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes of electron apps (teams, discord, slack, etc.). This could be a potential sign of ".asar" file tampering (See reference section for more information) or binary execution proxy through specific CLI arguments (see related rule)

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Electron Application CommandLine
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious CommandLine of electron apps (teams, discord, slack, etc.). This could be a sign of abuse to proxy execution through a signed binary.

rules.title: Hidden Powershell in Link File Pattern
rules.description: Detects events that appear when a user click on a link file with a powershell command in it

rules.title: Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 1
rules.description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

rules.title: Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 2
rules.description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

rules.title: Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 3
rules.description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

rules.title: Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 4
rules.description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

rules.title: ETW Logging Tamper In .NET Processes Via CommandLine
rules.description:
Detects changes to environment variables related to ETW logging via the CommandLine.
This could indicate potential adversaries stopping ETW providers recording loaded .NET assemblies.

rules.title: ETW Trace Evasion Activity
rules.description: Detects command line activity that tries to clear or disable any ETW trace log which could be a sign of logging evasion.

rules.title: Suspicious Eventlog Clearing or Configuration Change Activity
rules.description:
Detects the clearing or configuration tampering of EventLog using utilities such as "wevtutil", "powershell" and "wmic".
This technique were seen used by threat actors and ransomware strains in order to evade defenses.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious EventLog Recon Activity Using Log Query Utilities
rules.description:
Detects execution of different log query utilities and commands to search and dump the content of specific event logs or look for specific event IDs.
This technique is used by threat actors in order to extract sensitive information from events logs such as usernames, IP addresses, hostnames, etc.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Execution From Parent Process In Public Folder
rules.description: Detects a potentially suspicious execution of a parent process located in the "\Users\Public" folder executing a child process containing references to shell or scripting binaries and commandlines.

rules.title: Process Execution From A Potentially Suspicious Folder
rules.description: Detects a potentially suspicious execution from an uncommon folder.

rules.title: Suspicious Reconnaissance Activity Via GatherNetworkInfo.VBS
rules.description: Detects execution of the built-in script located in "C:\Windows\System32\gatherNetworkInfo.vbs". Which can be used to gather information about the target machine

rules.title: Potential Hidden Directory Creation Via NTFS INDEX_ALLOCATION Stream - CLI
rules.description: Detects command line containing reference to the "::$index_allocation" stream, which can be used as a technique to prevent access to folders or files from tooling such as "explorer.exe" or "powershell.exe"

rules.title: Writing Of Malicious Files To The Fonts Folder
rules.description: Monitors for the hiding possible malicious files in the C:\Windows\Fonts\ location. This folder doesn't require admin privillege to be written and executed from.

rules.title: Potential Homoglyph Attack Using Lookalike Characters
rules.description:
Detects the presence of unicode characters which are homoglyphs, or identical in appearance, to ASCII letter characters.
This is used as an obfuscation and masquerading techniques. Only "perfect" homoglyphs are included; these are characters that
are indistinguishable from ASCII characters and thus may make excellent candidates for homoglyph attack characters.

rules.title: Execution Of Non-Existing File
rules.description: Checks whether the image specified in a process creation event is not a full, absolute path (caused by process ghosting or other unorthodox methods to start a process)

rules.title: Base64 MZ Header In CommandLine
rules.description: Detects encoded base64 MZ header in the commandline

rules.title: Potential WinAPI Calls Via CommandLine
rules.description: Detects the use of WinAPI Functions via the commandline. As seen used by threat actors via the tool winapiexec

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious JWT Token Search Via CLI
rules.description:
Detects potentially suspicious search for JWT tokens via CLI by looking for the string "eyJ0eX" or "eyJhbG".
JWT tokens are often used for access-tokens across various applications and services like Microsoft 365, Azure, AWS, Google Cloud, and others.
Threat actors may search for these tokens to steal them for lateral movement or privilege escalation.

rules.title: Local Accounts Discovery
rules.description: Local accounts, System Owner/User discovery using operating systems utilities

rules.title: LOLBIN Execution From Abnormal Drive
rules.description: Detects LOLBINs executing from an abnormal or uncommon drive such as a mounted ISO.

rules.title: LSASS Dump Keyword In CommandLine
rules.description: Detects the presence of the keywords "lsass" and ".dmp" in the commandline, which could indicate a potential attempt to dump or create a dump of the lsass process.

rules.title: Potential File Download Via MS-AppInstaller Protocol Handler
rules.description:
Detects usage of the "ms-appinstaller" protocol handler via command line to potentially download arbitrary files via AppInstaller.EXE
The downloaded files are temporarly stored in ":\Users\%username%\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.DesktopAppInstaller_8wekyb3d8bbwe\AC\INetCache\<RANDOM-8-CHAR-DIRECTORY>"

rules.title: Suspicious Network Command
rules.description: Adversaries may look for details about the network configuration and settings of systems they access or through information discovery of remote systems

rules.title: Suspicious Scan Loop Network
rules.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of other systems by IP address, hostname, or other logical identifier on a network that may be used for Lateral Movement from the current system

rules.title: Potential Network Sniffing Activity Using Network Tools
rules.description:
Detects potential network sniffing via use of network tools such as "tshark", "windump".
Network sniffing refers to using the network interface on a system to monitor or capture information sent over a wired or wireless connection.
An adversary may place a network interface into promiscuous mode to passively access data in transit over the network, or use span ports to capture a larger amount of data.

rules.title: Process Launched Without Image Name
rules.description: Detect the use of processes with no name (".exe"), which can be used to evade Image-based detections.

rules.title: Execution of Suspicious File Type Extension
rules.description:
Detects whether the image specified in a process creation event doesn't refer to an ".exe" (or other known executable extension) file. This can be caused by process ghosting or other unorthodox methods to start a process.
This rule might require some initial baselining to align with some third party tooling in the user environment.

rules.title: Non-privileged Usage of Reg or Powershell
rules.description: Search for usage of reg or Powershell by non-privileged users to modify service configuration in registry

rules.title: Suspicious Process Patterns NTDS.DIT Exfil
rules.description: Detects suspicious process patterns used in NTDS.DIT exfiltration

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Call To Win32_NTEventlogFile Class
rules.description: Detects usage of the WMI class "Win32_NTEventlogFile" in a potentially suspicious way (delete, backup, change permissions, etc.) from a PowerShell script

rules.title: Use Short Name Path in Image
rules.description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid Image detection

rules.title: Use NTFS Short Name in Command Line
rules.description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid command-line detection

rules.title: Use NTFS Short Name in Image
rules.description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid Image based detection

rules.title: Obfuscated IP Download Activity
rules.description: Detects use of an encoded/obfuscated version of an IP address (hex, octal...) in an URL combined with a download command

rules.title: Obfuscated IP Via CLI
rules.description: Detects usage of an encoded/obfuscated version of an IP address (hex, octal, etc.) via command line

rules.title: Suspicious Process Parents
rules.description: Detects suspicious parent processes that should not have any children or should only have a single possible child program

rules.title: Potential PowerShell Execution Via DLL
rules.description:
Detects potential PowerShell execution from a DLL instead of the usual PowerShell process as seen used in PowerShdll.
This detection assumes that PowerShell commands are passed via the CommandLine.

rules.title: Privilege Escalation via Named Pipe Impersonation
rules.description: Detects a remote file copy attempt to a hidden network share. This may indicate lateral movement or data staging activity.

rules.title: Private Keys Reconnaissance Via CommandLine Tools
rules.description: Adversaries may search for private key certificate files on compromised systems for insecurely stored credential

rules.title: Suspicious RunAs-Like Flag Combination
rules.description: Detects suspicious command line flags that let the user set a target user and command as e.g. seen in PsExec-like tools

rules.title: Windows Processes Suspicious Parent Directory
rules.description: Detect suspicious parent processes of well-known Windows processes

rules.title: Suspicious Program Names
rules.description: Detects suspicious patterns in program names or folders that are often found in malicious samples or hacktools

rules.title: Recon Information for Export with Command Prompt
rules.description: Once established within a system or network, an adversary may use automated techniques for collecting internal data.

rules.title: Suspicious Process Execution From Fake Recycle.Bin Folder
rules.description: Detects process execution from a fake recycle bin folder, often used to avoid security solution.

rules.title: Suspicious Redirection to Local Admin Share
rules.description: Detects a suspicious output redirection to the local admins share, this technique is often found in malicious scripts or hacktool stagers

rules.title: Registry Modification of MS-settings Protocol Handler
rules.description:
Detects registry modifications to the 'ms-settings' protocol handler, which is frequently targeted for UAC bypass or persistence.
Attackers can modify this registry to execute malicious code with elevated privileges by hijacking the command execution path.

rules.title: Potential Remote Desktop Tunneling
rules.description: Detects potential use of an SSH utility to establish RDP over a reverse SSH Tunnel. This can be used by attackers to enable routing of network packets that would otherwise not reach their intended destination.

rules.title: Potential Defense Evasion Via Right-to-Left Override
rules.description:
Detects the presence of the "u202+E" character, which causes a terminal, browser, or operating system to render text in a right-to-left sequence.
This character is used as an obfuscation and masquerading techniques by adversaries to trick users into opening malicious files.

rules.title: Script Interpreter Execution From Suspicious Folder
rules.description: Detects a suspicious script execution in temporary folders or folders accessible by environment variables

rules.title: Suspicious Script Execution From Temp Folder
rules.description: Detects a suspicious script executions from temporary folder

rules.title: Sensitive File Access Via Volume Shadow Copy Backup
rules.description: Detects a command that accesses the VolumeShadowCopy in order to extract sensitive files such as the Security or SAM registry hives or the AD database (ntds.dit)

rules.title: Suspicious New Service Creation
rules.description: Detects creation of a new service via "sc" command or the powershell "new-service" cmdlet with suspicious binary paths

rules.title: Suspicious Service Binary Directory
rules.description: Detects a service binary running in a suspicious directory

rules.title: Suspicious Windows Service Tampering
rules.description: Detects the usage of binaries such as 'net', 'sc' or 'powershell' in order to stop, pause, disable or delete critical or important Windows services such as AV, Backup, etc. As seen being used in some ransomware scripts

values: Avast Business Console Client Antivirus Service

rules.title: Shadow Copies Creation Using Operating Systems Utilities
rules.description: Shadow Copies creation using operating systems utilities, possible credential access

rules.title: Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
rules.description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities

rules.title: Windows Shell/Scripting Processes Spawning Suspicious Programs
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes of a Windows shell and scripting processes such as wscript, rundll32, powershell, mshta...etc.

rules.title: Process Creation Using Sysnative Folder
rules.description: Detects process creation events that use the Sysnative folder (common for CobaltStrike spawns)

rules.title: System File Execution Location Anomaly
rules.description: Detects the execution of a Windows system binary that is usually located in the system folder from an uncommon location.

rules.title: Suspicious SYSTEM User Process Creation
rules.description: Detects a suspicious process creation as SYSTEM user (suspicious program or command line parameter)

rules.title: Suspicious SYSVOL Domain Group Policy Access
rules.description: Detects Access to Domain Group Policies stored in SYSVOL

rules.title: Tasks Folder Evasion
rules.description:
The Tasks folder in system32 and syswow64 are globally writable paths.
Adversaries can take advantage of this and load or influence any script hosts or ANY .NET Application
in Tasks to load and execute a custom assembly into cscript, wscript, regsvr32, mshta, eventvwr

rules.title: Malicious Windows Script Components File Execution by TAEF Detection
rules.description:
Windows Test Authoring and Execution Framework (TAEF) framework allows you to run automation by executing tests files written on different languages (C, C#, Microsoft COM Scripting interfaces
Adversaries may execute malicious code (such as WSC file with VBScript, dll and so on) directly by running te.exe

rules.title: Malicious PE Execution by Microsoft Visual Studio Debugger
rules.description:
There is an option for a MS VS Just-In-Time Debugger "vsjitdebugger.exe" to launch specified executable and attach a debugger.
This option may be used adversaries to execute malicious code by signed verified binary.
The debugger is installed alongside with Microsoft Visual Studio package.

rules.title: Suspicious Userinit Child Process
rules.description: Detects a suspicious child process of userinit

rules.title: Weak or Abused Passwords In CLI
rules.description:
Detects weak passwords or often abused passwords (seen used by threat actors) via the CLI.
An example would be a threat actor creating a new user via the net command and providing the password inline

rules.title: Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets
rules.description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via CommandLine

rules.title: WhoAmI as Parameter
rules.description: Detects a suspicious process command line that uses whoami as first parameter (as e.g. used by EfsPotato)

rules.title: Execution via WorkFolders.exe
rules.description: Detects using WorkFolders.exe to execute an arbitrary control.exe

rules.title: Suspect Svchost Activity
rules.description: It is extremely abnormal for svchost.exe to spawn without any CLI arguments and is normally observed when a malicious process spawns the process and injects code into the process memory space.

rules.title: Suspicious Process Masquerading As SvcHost.EXE
rules.description:
Detects a suspicious process that is masquerading as the legitimate "svchost.exe" by naming its binary "svchost.exe" and executing from an uncommon location.
Adversaries often disguise their malicious binaries by naming them after legitimate system processes like "svchost.exe" to evade detection.

rules.title: Terminal Service Process Spawn
rules.description: Detects a process spawned by the terminal service server process (this could be an indicator for an exploitation of CVE-2019-0708)

rules.title: Uncommon Svchost Parent Process
rules.description: Detects an uncommon svchost parent process

rules.title: Permission Check Via Accesschk.EXE
rules.description: Detects the usage of the "Accesschk" utility, an access and privilege audit tool developed by SysInternal and often being abused by attacker to verify process privileges

rules.title: Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
rules.description: Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.

rules.title: Suspicious Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
rules.description: Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database to a suspicious directory. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.

rules.title: Potential Execution of Sysinternals Tools
rules.description: Detects command lines that contain the 'accepteula' flag which could be a sign of execution of one of the Sysinternals tools

rules.title: Potential Memory Dumping Activity Via LiveKD
rules.description: Detects execution of LiveKD based on PE metadata or image name

rules.title: Kernel Memory Dump Via LiveKD
rules.description: Detects execution of LiveKD with the "-m" flag to potentially dump the kernel memory

rules.title: Procdump Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of the SysInternals Procdump utility

rules.title: Potential SysInternals ProcDump Evasion
rules.description: Detects uses of the SysInternals ProcDump utility in which ProcDump or its output get renamed, or a dump file is moved or copied to a different name

rules.title: Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump
rules.description:
Detects potential credential harvesting attempts through LSASS memory dumps using ProcDump.
This rule identifies suspicious command-line patterns that combine memory dump flags (-ma, -mm, -mp) with LSASS-related process markers.
LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) contains sensitive authentication data including plaintext passwords, NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets in memory.
Attackers commonly dump LSASS memory to extract credentials for lateral movement and privilege escalation.

rules.title: Psexec Execution
rules.description: Detects user accept agreement execution in psexec commandline

rules.title: PsExec/PAExec Escalation to LOCAL SYSTEM
rules.description: Detects suspicious commandline flags used by PsExec and PAExec to escalate a command line to LOCAL_SYSTEM rights

rules.title: Potential PsExec Remote Execution
rules.description: Detects potential psexec command that initiate execution on a remote systems via common commandline flags used by the utility

rules.title: PsExec Service Execution
rules.description: Detects launch of the PSEXESVC service, which means that this system was the target of a psexec remote execution

rules.title: PsExec Service Child Process Execution as LOCAL SYSTEM
rules.description: Detects suspicious launch of the PSEXESVC service on this system and a sub process run as LOCAL_SYSTEM (-s), which means that someone remotely started a command on this system running it with highest privileges and not only the privileges of the login user account (e.g. the administrator account)

rules.title: Suspicious Use of PsLogList
rules.description: Detects usage of the PsLogList utility to dump event log in order to extract admin accounts and perform account discovery or delete events logs

rules.title: Sysinternals PsService Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of Sysinternals PsService which can be abused for service reconnaissance and tampering

rules.title: Sysinternals PsSuspend Execution
rules.description: Detects usage of Sysinternals PsSuspend which can be abused to suspend critical processes

rules.title: Sysinternals PsSuspend Suspicious Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious execution of Sysinternals PsSuspend, where the utility is used to suspend critical processes such as AV or EDR to bypass defenses

rules.title: Potential File Overwrite Via Sysinternals SDelete
rules.description: Detects the use of SDelete to erase a file not the free space

rules.title: Potential Privilege Escalation To LOCAL SYSTEM
rules.description: Detects unknown program using commandline flags usually used by tools such as PsExec and PAExec to start programs with SYSTEM Privileges

rules.title: Sysmon Configuration Update
rules.description: Detects updates to Sysmon's configuration. Attackers might update or replace the Sysmon configuration with a bare bone one to avoid monitoring without shutting down the service completely

rules.title: Uninstall Sysinternals Sysmon
rules.description: Detects the removal of Sysmon, which could be a potential attempt at defense evasion

rules.title: Potential Binary Impersonating Sysinternals Tools
rules.description:
Detects binaries that use the same name as legitimate sysinternals tools to evade detection.
This rule looks for the execution of binaries that are named similarly to Sysinternals tools.
Adversary may rename their malicious tools as legitimate Sysinternals tools to evade detection.

rules.title: Sysprep on AppData Folder
rules.description: Detects suspicious sysprep process start with AppData folder as target (as used by Trojan Syndicasec in Thrip report by Symantec)

rules.title: Suspicious Execution of Systeminfo
rules.description: Detects usage of the "systeminfo" command to retrieve information

rules.title: Potential Signing Bypass Via Windows Developer Features
rules.description: Detects when a user enable developer features such as "Developer Mode" or "Application Sideloading". Which allows the user to install untrusted packages.

rules.title: Suspicious Recursive Takeown
rules.description: Adversaries can interact with the DACLs using built-in Windows commands takeown which can grant adversaries higher permissions on specific files and folders

rules.title: Tap Installer Execution
rules.description: Well-known TAP software installation. Possible preparation for data exfiltration using tunneling techniques

rules.title: Compressed File Creation Via Tar.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of "tar.exe" in order to create a compressed file.
Adversaries may abuse various utilities to compress or encrypt data before exfiltration.

rules.title: Compressed File Extraction Via Tar.EXE
rules.description:
Detects execution of "tar.exe" in order to extract compressed file.
Adversaries may abuse various utilities in order to decompress data to avoid detection.

rules.title: Taskkill Symantec Endpoint Protection
rules.description:
Detects one of the possible scenarios for disabling Symantec Endpoint Protection.
Symantec Endpoint Protection antivirus software services incorrectly implement the protected service mechanism.
As a result, the NT AUTHORITY/SYSTEM user can execute the taskkill /im command several times ccSvcHst.exe /f, thereby killing the process belonging to the service, and thus shutting down the service.

rules.title: Loaded Module Enumeration Via Tasklist.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the enumeration of a specific DLL or EXE being used by a binary via "tasklist.exe".
This is often used by attackers in order to find the specific process identifier (PID) that is using the DLL in question.
In order to dump the process memory or perform other nefarious actions.

rules.title: Taskmgr as LOCAL_SYSTEM
rules.description: Detects the creation of taskmgr.exe process in context of LOCAL_SYSTEM

rules.title: New Process Created Via Taskmgr.EXE
rules.description: Detects the creation of a process via the Windows task manager. This might be an attempt to bypass UAC

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Command Targeting Teams Sensitive Files
rules.description:
Detects a commandline containing references to the Microsoft Teams database or cookies files from a process other than Teams.
The database might contain authentication tokens and other sensitive information about the logged in accounts.

rules.title: New Virtual Smart Card Created Via TpmVscMgr.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "Tpmvscmgr.exe" to create a new virtual smart card.

rules.title: Suspicious TSCON Start as SYSTEM
rules.description: Detects a tscon.exe start as LOCAL SYSTEM

rules.title: Suspicious RDP Redirect Using TSCON
rules.description: Detects a suspicious RDP session redirect using tscon.exe

rules.title: Potential RDP Session Hijacking Activity
rules.description: Detects potential RDP Session Hijacking activity on Windows systems

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using ChangePK and SLUI
rules.description: Detects an UAC bypass that uses changepk.exe and slui.exe (UACMe 61)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using Disk Cleanup
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using scheduled tasks and variable expansion of cleanmgr.exe (UACMe 34)

rules.title: Bypass UAC via CMSTP
rules.description: Detect commandline usage of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer (cmstp.exe) to install specially formatted local .INF files

rules.title: CMSTP UAC Bypass via COM Object Access
rules.description: Detects UAC Bypass Attempt Using Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer Autoelevate-capable COM Objects (e.g. UACMe ID of 41, 43, 58 or 65)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Tools Using ComputerDefaults
rules.description: Detects tools such as UACMe used to bypass UAC with computerdefaults.exe (UACMe 59)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using Consent and Comctl32 - Process
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using consent.exe and comctl32.dll (UACMe 22)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using DismHost
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using DismHost DLL hijacking (UACMe 63)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using Event Viewer RecentViews
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using Event Viewer RecentViews

rules.title: Bypass UAC via Fodhelper.exe
rules.description: Identifies use of Fodhelper.exe to bypass User Account Control. Adversaries use this technique to execute privileged processes.

rules.title: UAC Bypass via Windows Firewall Snap-In Hijack
rules.description: Detects attempts to bypass User Account Control (UAC) by hijacking the Microsoft Management Console (MMC) Windows Firewall snap-in

rules.title: UAC Bypass via ICMLuaUtil
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using ICMLuaUtil Elevated COM interface

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using IDiagnostic Profile
rules.description: Detects the "IDiagnosticProfileUAC" UAC bypass technique

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using IEInstal - Process
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using IEInstal.exe (UACMe 64)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using MSConfig Token Modification - Process
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using a msconfig GUI hack (UACMe 55)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using NTFS Reparse Point - Process
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using NTFS reparse point and wusa.exe DLL hijacking (UACMe 36)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using PkgMgr and DISM
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using pkgmgr.exe and dism.exe (UACMe 23)

rules.title: Potential UAC Bypass Via Sdclt.EXE
rules.description: A General detection for sdclt being spawned as an elevated process. This could be an indicator of sdclt being used for bypass UAC techniques.

rules.title: TrustedPath UAC Bypass Pattern
rules.description: Detects indicators of a UAC bypass method by mocking directories

rules.title: UAC Bypass Abusing Winsat Path Parsing - Process
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using a path parsing issue in winsat.exe (UACMe 52)

rules.title: UAC Bypass Using Windows Media Player - Process
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using Windows Media Player osksupport.dll (UACMe 32)

rules.title: Bypass UAC via WSReset.exe
rules.description: Detects use of WSReset.exe to bypass User Account Control (UAC). Adversaries use this technique to execute privileged processes.

rules.title: UAC Bypass WSReset
rules.description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass via WSReset usable by default sysmon-config

rules.title: Use of UltraVNC Remote Access Software
rules.description: An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software,to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks

rules.title: Suspicious UltraVNC Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious UltraVNC command line flag combination that indicate a auto reconnect upon execution, e.g. startup (as seen being used by Gamaredon threat group)

rules.title: Uninstall Crowdstrike Falcon Sensor
rules.description: Adversaries may disable security tools to avoid possible detection of their tools and activities by uninstalling Crowdstrike Falcon

rules.title: Uncommon Userinit Child Process
rules.description: Detects uncommon "userinit.exe" child processes, which could be a sign of uncommon shells or login scripts used for persistence.

rules.title: Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd
rules.description: List credentials currently stored in Windows Credential Manager via the native Windows utility vaultcmd.exe

rules.title: Verclsid.exe Runs COM Object
rules.description: Detects when verclsid.exe is used to run COM object via GUID

rules.title: Virtualbox Driver Installation or Starting of VMs
rules.description: Adversaries can carry out malicious operations using a virtual instance to avoid detection. This rule is built to detect the registration of the Virtualbox driver or start of a Virtualbox VM.

rules.title: Suspicious VBoxDrvInst.exe Parameters
rules.description:
Detect VBoxDrvInst.exe run with parameters allowing processing INF file.
This allows to create values in the registry and install drivers.
For example one could use this technique to obtain persistence via modifying one of Run or RunOnce registry keys

rules.title: Potential Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
rules.description: Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script to run for a specific VM state

rules.title: Suspicious Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
rules.description: Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script that's located in a potentially suspicious location to run for a specific VM state

rules.title: VMToolsd Suspicious Child Process
rules.description: Detects suspicious child process creations of VMware Tools process which may indicate persistence setup

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of VsCode
rules.description: Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes spawning from a VsCode "code.exe" process. This could indicate an attempt of persistence via VsCode tasks or terminal profiles.

rules.title: Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
rules.description: Detects Visual Studio Code tunnel execution. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel

rules.title: Visual Studio Code Tunnel Shell Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of a shell (powershell, bash, wsl...) via Visual Studio Code tunnel. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel and execute arbitrary commands on the system.

rules.title: Renamed Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
rules.description: Detects renamed Visual Studio Code tunnel execution. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel

rules.title: Visual Studio Code Tunnel Service Installation
rules.description: Detects the installation of VsCode tunnel (code-tunnel) as a service.

rules.title: Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via VSDiagnostics.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "VSDiagnostics.exe" with the "start" command in order to launch and proxy arbitrary binaries.

rules.title: Suspicious Vsls-Agent Command With AgentExtensionPath Load
rules.description: Detects Microsoft Visual Studio vsls-agent.exe lolbin execution with a suspicious library load using the --agentExtensionPath parameter

rules.title: Use of W32tm as Timer
rules.description: When configured with suitable command line arguments, w32tm can act as a delay mechanism

rules.title: Wab Execution From Non Default Location
rules.description: Detects execution of wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) from non default locations as seen with bumblebee activity

rules.title: Wab/Wabmig Unusual Parent Or Child Processes
rules.description: Detects unusual parent or children of the wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) processes as seen being used with bumblebee activity

rules.title: All Backups Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the deletion of all backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe".
This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors.
This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.

rules.title: Windows Backup Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the deletion of backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe".
This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors.
This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.

rules.title: Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.

rules.title: File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the recovery of files from backups via "wbadmin.exe".
Attackers can restore sensitive files such as NTDS.DIT or Registry Hives from backups in order to potentially extract credentials.

rules.title: Sensitive File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious WebDAV LNK Execution
rules.description: Detects possible execution via LNK file accessed on a WebDAV server.

rules.title: Chopper Webshell Process Pattern
rules.description: Detects patterns found in process executions cause by China Chopper like tiny (ASPX) webshells

rules.title: Webshell Hacking Activity Patterns
rules.description: Detects certain parent child patterns found in cases in which a web shell is used to perform certain credential dumping or exfiltration activities on a compromised system

rules.title: Webshell Detection With Command Line Keywords
rules.description: Detects certain command line parameters often used during reconnaissance activity via web shells

rules.title: Suspicious Process By Web Server Process
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious processes being spawned by a web server process which could be the result of a successfully placed web shell or exploitation

rules.title: Webshell Tool Reconnaissance Activity
rules.description: Detects processes spawned from web servers (PHP, Tomcat, IIS, etc.) that perform reconnaissance looking for the existence of popular scripting tools (perl, python, wget) on the system via the help commands

rules.title: Potential ReflectDebugger Content Execution Via WerFault.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of "WerFault.exe" with the "-pr" commandline flag that is used to run files stored in the ReflectDebugger key which could be used to store the path to the malware in order to masquerade the execution flow

rules.title: Suspicious Child Process Of Wermgr.EXE
rules.description: Detects suspicious Windows Error Reporting manager (wermgr.exe) child process

rules.title: Suspicious Execution Location Of Wermgr.EXE
rules.description: Detects suspicious Windows Error Reporting manager (wermgr.exe) execution location.

rules.title: Suspicious File Download From IP Via Wget.EXE
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious file downloads directly from IP addresses using Wget.exe

rules.title: Suspicious File Download From File Sharing Domain Via Wget.EXE
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious file downloads from file sharing domains using wget.exe

rules.title: Suspicious File Download From IP Via Wget.EXE - Paths
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious file downloads directly from IP addresses and stored in suspicious locations using Wget.exe

rules.title: Suspicious Where Execution
rules.description:
Adversaries may enumerate browser bookmarks to learn more about compromised hosts.
Browser bookmarks may reveal personal information about users (ex: banking sites, interests, social media, etc.) as well as details about
internal network resources such as servers, tools/dashboards, or other related infrastructure.

rules.title: Enumerate All Information With Whoami.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of "whoami.exe" with the "/all" flag

rules.title: Whoami.EXE Execution From Privileged Process
rules.description: Detects the execution of "whoami.exe" by privileged accounts that are often abused by threat actors

rules.title: Group Membership Reconnaissance Via Whoami.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of whoami.exe with the /group command line flag to show group membership for the current user, account type, security identifiers (SID), and attributes.

rules.title: Whoami.EXE Execution With Output Option
rules.description: Detects the execution of "whoami.exe" with the "/FO" flag to choose CSV as output format or with redirection options to export the results to a file for later use.

rules.title: Whoami.EXE Execution Anomaly
rules.description: Detects the execution of whoami.exe with suspicious parent processes.

rules.title: Security Privileges Enumeration Via Whoami.EXE
rules.description: Detects a whoami.exe executed with the /priv command line flag instructing the tool to show all current user privileges. This is often used after a privilege escalation attempt.

rules.title: Suspicious WindowsTerminal Child Processes
rules.description: Detects suspicious children spawned via the Windows Terminal application which could be a sign of persistence via WindowsTerminal (see references section)

rules.title: Add New Download Source To Winget
rules.description: Detects usage of winget to add new additional download sources

rules.title: Add Insecure Download Source To Winget
rules.description:
Detects usage of winget to add a new insecure (http) download source.
Winget will not allow the addition of insecure sources, hence this could indicate potential suspicious activity (or typos)

rules.title: Add Potential Suspicious New Download Source To Winget
rules.description: Detects usage of winget to add new potentially suspicious download sources

rules.title: Install New Package Via Winget Local Manifest
rules.description:
Detects usage of winget to install applications via manifest file. Adversaries can abuse winget to download payloads remotely and execute them.
The manifest option enables you to install an application by passing in a YAML file directly to the client.
Winget can be used to download and install exe, msi or msix files later.

rules.title: Winrar Compressing Dump Files
rules.description: Detects execution of WinRAR in order to compress a file with a ".dmp"/".dump" extension, which could be a step in a process of dump file exfiltration.

rules.title: Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of WinRAR.EXE
rules.description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of WinRAR.exe.

rules.title: WinRAR Execution in Non-Standard Folder
rules.description: Detects a suspicious WinRAR execution in a folder which is not the default installation folder

rules.title: AWL Bypass with Winrm.vbs and Malicious WsmPty.xsl/WsmTxt.xsl
rules.description: Detects execution of attacker-controlled WsmPty.xsl or WsmTxt.xsl via winrm.vbs and copied cscript.exe (can be renamed)

rules.title: Remote Code Execute via Winrm.vbs
rules.description: Detects an attempt to execute code or create service on remote host via winrm.vbs.

rules.title: Remote PowerShell Session Host Process (WinRM)
rules.description: Detects remote PowerShell sections by monitoring for wsmprovhost (WinRM host process) as a parent or child process (sign of an active PowerShell remote session).

rules.title: Suspicious Processes Spawned by WinRM
rules.description: Detects suspicious processes including shells spawnd from WinRM host process

rules.title: Compress Data and Lock With Password for Exfiltration With WINZIP
rules.description: An adversary may compress or encrypt data that is collected prior to exfiltration using 3rd party utilities

rules.title: WMI Backdoor Exchange Transport Agent
rules.description: Detects a WMI backdoor in Exchange Transport Agents via WMI event filters

rules.title: WMI Persistence - Script Event Consumer
rules.description: Detects WMI script event consumers

rules.title: New ActiveScriptEventConsumer Created Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects WMIC executions in which an event consumer gets created. This could be used to establish persistence

rules.title: Potential Windows Defender Tampering Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects potential tampering with Windows Defender settings such as adding exclusion using wmic

rules.title: New Process Created Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects new process creation using WMIC via the "process call create" flag

rules.title: Computer System Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects execution of wmic utility with the "computersystem" flag in order to obtain information about the machine such as the domain, username, model, etc.

rules.title: Hardware Model Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of WMIC with the "csproduct" which is used to obtain information such as hardware models and vendor information

rules.title: Local Groups Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of "wmic" with the "group" flag.
Adversaries may attempt to find local system groups and permission settings.
The knowledge of local system permission groups can help adversaries determine which groups exist and which users belong to a particular group.
Adversaries may use this information to determine which users have elevated permissions, such as the users found within the local administrators group.

rules.title: Windows Hotfix Updates Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of wmic with the "qfe" flag in order to obtain information about installed hotfix updates on the system. This is often used by pentester and attacker enumeration scripts

rules.title: Process Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of "wmic" with the "process" flag, which adversary might use to list processes running on the compromised host or list installed software hotfixes and patches.

rules.title: Potential Product Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects the execution of WMIC in order to get a list of firewall and antivirus products

rules.title: Potential Product Class Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of WMIC in order to get a list of firewall, antivirus and antispywware products.
Adversaries often enumerate security products installed on a system to identify security controls and potential ways to evade detection or disable protection mechanisms.
This information helps them plan their next attack steps and choose appropriate techniques to bypass security measures.

rules.title: Service Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description:
An adversary might use WMI to check if a certain remote service is running on a remote device.
When the test completes, a service information will be displayed on the screen if it exists.
A common feedback message is that "No instance(s) Available" if the service queried is not running.
A common error message is "Node - (provided IP or default) ERROR Description =The RPC server is unavailable" if the provided remote host is unreachable

rules.title: Uncommon System Information Discovery Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the use of the WMI command-line (WMIC) utility to identify and display various system information,
including OS, CPU, GPU, and disk drive names; memory capacity; display resolution; and baseboard, BIOS,
and GPU driver products/versions.
Some of these commands were used by Aurora Stealer in late 2022/early 2023.

rules.title: Potential Unquoted Service Path Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects known WMI recon method to look for unquoted service paths using wmic. Often used by pentester and attacker enumeration scripts

rules.title: System Disk And Volume Reconnaissance Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description:
An adversary might use WMI to discover information about the system, such as the volume name, size,
free space, and other disk information. This can be done using the 'wmic' command-line utility and has been
observed being used by threat actors such as Volt Typhoon.

rules.title: WMIC Remote Command Execution
rules.description: Detects the execution of WMIC to query information on a remote system

rules.title: Service Started/Stopped Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects usage of wmic to start or stop a service

rules.title: Potential Remote SquiblyTwo Technique Execution
rules.description:
Detects potential execution of the SquiblyTwo technique that leverages Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI)
to execute malicious code remotely. This technique bypasses application whitelisting by using wmic.exe to process
malicious XSL (eXtensible Stylesheet Language) scripts that can contain embedded JScript or VBScript.
The attack typically works by fetching XSL content from a remote source (using HTTP/HTTPS) and executing it
with full trust privileges directly in memory, avoiding disk-based detection mechanisms. This is a common
LOLBin (Living Off The Land Binary) technique used for defense evasion and code execution.

rules.title: Suspicious WMIC Execution Via Office Process
rules.description: Office application called wmic to proxye execution through a LOLBIN process. This is often used to break suspicious parent-child chain (Office app spawns LOLBin).

rules.title: Suspicious Process Created Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects WMIC executing "process call create" with suspicious calls to processes such as "rundll32", "regsrv32", etc.

rules.title: Application Terminated Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects calls to the "terminate" function via wmic in order to kill an application

rules.title: Application Removed Via Wmic.EXE
rules.description: Detects the removal or uninstallation of an application via "Wmic.EXE".

rules.title: Potential Tampering With Security Products Via WMIC
rules.description: Detects uninstallation or termination of security products using the WMIC utility

rules.title: XSL Script Execution Via WMIC.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of WMIC with the "format" flag to potentially load local XSL files.
Adversaries abuse this functionality to execute arbitrary files while potentially bypassing application whitelisting defenses.
Extensible Stylesheet Language (XSL) files are commonly used to describe the processing and rendering of data within XML files.

rules.title: Potential WMI Lateral Movement WmiPrvSE Spawned PowerShell
rules.description: Detects Powershell as a child of the WmiPrvSE process. Which could be a sign of lateral movement via WMI.

rules.title: Suspicious WmiPrvSE Child Process
rules.description: Detects suspicious and uncommon child processes of WmiPrvSE

rules.title: UEFI Persistence Via Wpbbin - ProcessCreation
rules.description: Detects execution of the binary "wpbbin" which is used as part of the UEFI based persistence method described in the reference section

rules.title: Potential Dropper Script Execution Via WScript/CScript
rules.description: Detects wscript/cscript executions of scripts located in user directories

rules.title: Cscript/Wscript Potentially Suspicious Child Process
rules.description:
Detects potentially suspicious child processes of Wscript/Cscript. These include processes such as rundll32 with uncommon exports or PowerShell spawning rundll32 or regsvr32.
Malware such as Pikabot and Qakbot were seen using similar techniques as well as many others.

rules.title: Cscript/Wscript Uncommon Script Extension Execution
rules.description: Detects Wscript/Cscript executing a file with an uncommon (i.e. non-script) extension

rules.title: WSL Child Process Anomaly
rules.description: Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes spawning from a WSL process. This could indicate an attempt to evade parent/child relationship detections or persistence attempts via cron using WSL

rules.title: Windows Binary Executed From WSL
rules.description:
Detects the execution of Windows binaries from within a WSL instance.
This could be used to masquerade parent-child relationships

rules.title: Proxy Execution Via Wuauclt.EXE
rules.description: Detects the use of the Windows Update Client binary (wuauclt.exe) for proxy execution.

rules.title: Suspicious Windows Update Agent Empty Cmdline
rules.description: Detects suspicious Windows Update Agent activity in which a wuauclt.exe process command line doesn't contain any command line flags

rules.title: Cab File Extraction Via Wusa.EXE From Potentially Suspicious Paths
rules.description: Detects the execution of the "wusa.exe" (Windows Update Standalone Installer) utility to extract ".cab" files using the "/extract" argument from potentially suspicious paths.

rules.title: Wusa.EXE Executed By Parent Process Located In Suspicious Location
rules.description:
Detects execution of the "wusa.exe" (Windows Update Standalone Installer) utility by a parent process that is located in a suspicious location.
Attackers could instantiate an instance of "wusa.exe" in order to bypass User Account Control (UAC). They can duplicate the access token from "wusa.exe" to gain elevated privileges.

rules.title: Xwizard.EXE Execution From Non-Default Location
rules.description:
Detects the execution of Xwizard tool from a non-default directory.
When executed from a non-default directory, this utility can be abused in order to side load a custom version of "xwizards.dll".

rules.title: COM Object Execution via Xwizard.EXE
rules.description:
Detects the execution of Xwizard tool with the "RunWizard" flag and a GUID like argument.
This utility can be abused in order to run custom COM object created in the registry.

rules.title: Creation of a Local Hidden User Account by Registry
rules.description: Sysmon registry detection of a local hidden user account.

rules.title: UAC Bypass Via Wsreset
rules.description: Unfixed method for UAC bypass from Windows 10. WSReset.exe file associated with the Windows Store. It will run a binary file contained in a low-privilege registry.

rules.title: CMSTP Execution Registry Event
rules.description: Detects various indicators of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer execution

rules.title: Wdigest CredGuard Registry Modification
rules.description:
Detects potential malicious modification of the property value of IsCredGuardEnabled from
HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest to disable Cred Guard on a system.
This is usually used with UseLogonCredential to manipulate the caching credentials.

rules.title: Esentutl Volume Shadow Copy Service Keys
rules.description: Detects the volume shadow copy service initialization and processing via esentutl. Registry keys such as HKLM\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\VSS\\Diag\\VolSnap\\Volume are captured.

rules.title: Windows Credential Editor Registry
rules.description: Detects the use of Windows Credential Editor (WCE)

rules.title: HybridConnectionManager Service Installation - Registry
rules.description: Detects the installation of the Azure Hybrid Connection Manager service to allow remote code execution from Azure function.

rules.title: Registry Entries For Azorult Malware
rules.description: Detects the presence of a registry key created during Azorult execution

rules.title: Potential Qakbot Registry Activity
rules.description: Detects a registry key used by IceID in a campaign that distributes malicious OneNote files

rules.title: Path To Screensaver Binary Modified
rules.description: Detects value modification of registry key containing path to binary used as screensaver.

rules.title: Narrator's Feedback-Hub Persistence
rules.description: Detects abusing Windows 10 Narrator's Feedback-Hub

rules.title: NetNTLM Downgrade Attack - Registry
rules.description: Detects NetNTLM downgrade attack

rules.title: Office Application Startup - Office Test
rules.description: Detects the addition of office test registry that allows a user to specify an arbitrary DLL that will be executed every time an Office application is started

rules.title: Windows Registry Trust Record Modification
rules.description: Alerts on trust record modification within the registry, indicating usage of macros

rules.title: New PortProxy Registry Entry Added
rules.description: Detects the modification of the PortProxy registry key which is used for port forwarding.

rules.title: RedMimicry Winnti Playbook Registry Manipulation
rules.description: Detects actions caused by the RedMimicry Winnti playbook

rules.title: WINEKEY Registry Modification
rules.description: Detects potential malicious modification of run keys by winekey or team9 backdoor

rules.title: Run Once Task Configuration in Registry
rules.description: Rule to detect the configuration of Run Once registry key. Configured payload can be run by runonce.exe /AlternateShellStartup

rules.title: Shell Open Registry Keys Manipulation
rules.description: Detects the shell open key manipulation (exefile and ms-settings) used for persistence and the pattern of UAC Bypass using fodhelper.exe, computerdefaults.exe, slui.exe via registry keys (e.g. UACMe 33 or 62)

rules.title: Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS SilentProcessExit Technique
rules.description: Detects changes to the Registry in which a monitor program gets registered to dump the memory of the lsass.exe process

rules.title: Security Support Provider (SSP) Added to LSA Configuration
rules.description: Detects the addition of a SSP to the registry. Upon a reboot or API call, SSP DLLs gain access to encrypted and plaintext passwords stored in Windows.

rules.title: Sticky Key Like Backdoor Usage - Registry
rules.description: Detects the usage and installation of a backdoor that uses an option to register a malicious debugger for built-in tools that are accessible in the login screen

rules.title: Atbroker Registry Change
rules.description: Detects creation/modification of Assistive Technology applications and persistence with usage of 'at'

rules.title: Suspicious Run Key from Download
rules.description: Detects the suspicious RUN keys created by software located in Download or temporary Outlook/Internet Explorer directories

rules.title: DLL Load via LSASS
rules.description: Detects a method to load DLL via LSASS process using an undocumented Registry key

rules.title: Suspicious Camera and Microphone Access
rules.description: Detects Processes accessing the camera and microphone from suspicious folder
